Zhi Faheng was very excited but could not ascend. He grew tired and returned to the lower place. They took him away. People laughed and said, "What did you see that you couldn't ascend?" Then they handed Zhi Faheng over to the ship officials. The ship officials were operating the ship and made him the helmsman. Zhi Faheng said, "I cannot hold the tiller." They forced him. There were hundreds of ships following behind him. Zhi Faheng did not know how to hold the tiller and crashed onto a sandbar. The officials pushed Zhi Faheng and said, "You led the ship astray, and according to the law, you should be executed." [14]

    引衡上岸，雷鼓将斩。忽有五色二龙，推船还浮。吏乃原衡罪。载衡北行。三十许里，见好屯岸，有数万家，云是流人。衡窃上岸。屯中饶狗，牙欲啮之。衡大恐惧。望见西北有讲堂，上有沙门甚众，闻经呗之声。衡遽走趣之。堂有十二阶。衡始蹑一阶，见亡师法柱踞胡床坐。见衡曰：“我弟子也，何以而来？”因起临阶，以手巾打衡面，曰，“莫来！”衡甚欲上，复举步登阶。柱复推令下。至三乃止。见平地有井一口，深三四丈，塼无隙际。衡心念言，此井自然。井边有人谓曰：“不自然者，何得成井？”虽见法柱，故倚望之，谓衡“可复道还去，狗不啮汝！”衡还水边，亦不见向来船也。衡渴欲饮水，乃堕水中，因便得稣。于是出家，持戒菜食。昼夜精思为至行。沙门比丘法桥，衡弟子也。珠林七 [15]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [15]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 引衡上岸 - Literally 'lead Heng onto the shore,' but likely means 'lead Heng to the shore' or 'take Heng to the shore.'
    • 雷鼓将斩 - 'Sound the drum as if for execution,' a dramatic expression indicating imminent punishment.
    • 五色二龙 - 'Two colorful dragons,' a vivid and symbolic description.
    • 推船还浮 - 'Push the boat back to float,' indicating the dragons' intervention.
    • 吏乃原衡罪 - 'The official then pardoned Heng's crime,' where '原' means 'to pardon' or 'to forgive.'
    • 载衡北行 - 'Take Heng northward,' indicating a journey.
    • 三十许里 - 'About thirty miles,' a rough estimate of distance.
    • 好屯岸 - 'A good harbor or landing place,' where '好' suggests a favorable location.
    • 流人 - 'Displaced people' or 'refugees,' referring to those who have been forced to move.
    • 牙欲啮之 - 'The dogs wanted to bite him,' indicating the dogs' aggressive behavior.
    • 讲堂 - 'Lecture hall' or 'lecture room,' a place for teaching.
    • 沙门甚众 - 'Many monks,' where '沙门' refers to Buddhist monks.
    • 经呗之声 - 'The sound of chanting scriptures,' where '经' refers to Buddhist scriptures and '呗' refers to chanting.
    • 遽走趣之 - 'Rushed to go there,' indicating a hurried action.
    • 堂有十二阶 - 'The hall had twelve steps,' a specific detail about the building.
    • 亡师法柱 - 'The deceased teacher Fa Zhu,' where '亡' means 'deceased' and '法柱' is the name of the teacher.
    • 踞胡床坐 - 'Sitting on a trapeze-like chair,' where '胡床' refers to a type of folding chair.
    • 见衡曰 - 'Saw Heng and said,' indicating a direct quote.
    • 莫来 - 'Do not come,' a command or warning.
    • 举步登阶 - 'Take steps to ascend the stairs,' indicating an attempt to climb.
    • 柱复推令下 - 'Fa Zhu pushed him down again,' indicating repeated attempts and failures.
    • 至三乃止 - 'Up to three times and then stopped,' indicating a limit to the attempts.
    • 平地有井一口 - 'A well on the ground,' a specific detail about the location.
    • 深三四丈 - 'About 30 to 40 feet deep,' a rough estimate of depth.
    • 塼无隙际 - 'The bricks have no gaps,' indicating a seamless construction.
    • 此井自然 - 'This well is natural,' suggesting that the well is not man-made.
    • 井边有人谓曰 - 'Someone by the well said,' indicating a direct quote.
    • 不自然者，何得成井 - 'If it's not natural, how could it be a well?' a rhetorical question.
    • 虽见法柱，故倚望之 - 'Even though he saw Fa Zhu, he still hoped,' indicating a lingering hope.
    • 可复道还去，狗不啮汝 - 'Can you go back, and the dogs won't bite you,' a suggestion or command.
    • 衡还水边 - 'Heng returned to the water's edge,' indicating a return to the original location.
    • 亦不见向来船也 - 'Nor did he see the boat he had come in,' indicating a disappearance.
    • 衡渴欲饮水 - 'Heng was thirsty and wanted to drink water,' indicating a physical need.
    • 乃堕水中 - 'Fell into the water,' indicating an accidental fall.
    • 因便得苏 - 'And thus revived,' indicating a recovery.
    • 于是出家 - 'He then became a monk,' indicating a significant life change.
    • 持戒菜食 - 'Observed precepts and ate vegetables,' indicating a strict religious practice.
    • 昼夜精思为至行 - 'Thought deeply day and night, practicing the highest virtues,' indicating a dedication to spiritual practice.
    • 沙门比丘法桥 - 'Monk Bhikshu Fa Qiao,' where '比丘' refers to a fully ordained monk and '法桥' is the name of the monk.
    • 衡弟子也 - 'Is Heng's disciple,' indicating a relationship.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 衡 - Likely refers to a person named Heng, possibly a historical figure or a character in a story.
    • 雷鼓 - A drum used in ancient China, often associated with military or ceremonial purposes.
    • 五色二龙 - Symbolic of divine or supernatural intervention, often associated with Buddhist or Taoist beliefs.
    • 吏 - Refers to an official or government representative.
    • 流人 - Displaced people, often refugees or those who have been forced to move due to political or social reasons.
    • 讲堂 - A place for teaching, often associated with Buddhist monasteries.
    • 沙门 - Buddhist monks, indicating a religious context.
    • 经呗 - Chanting of Buddhist scriptures, a common practice in Buddhist temples.
    • 亡师法柱 - A deceased teacher named Fa Zhu, likely a respected figure in the Buddhist community.
    • 胡床 - A type of folding chair, often used in ancient China.
    • 井 - Wells were important sources of water in ancient China, and the description of a natural well adds a mystical element.
    • 出家 - Becoming a monk, a significant life decision in Buddhist tradition.
    • 持戒菜食 - Observing precepts and eating a vegetarian diet, indicating a strict religious practice.
    • 昼夜精思为至行 - Deep meditation and spiritual practice, indicating a dedication to achieving higher spiritual goals.
    • 沙门比丘法桥 - A monk named Fa Qiao, likely a respected figure in the Buddhist community.
    • 衡弟子也 - Heng's disciple, indicating a relationship within the Buddhist community.

    Proper Nouns

    • 衡 - A person named Heng.
    • 法柱 - The name of the deceased teacher.
    • 法桥 - The name of the monk who is Heng's disciple.

    Lead Heng onto the shore, and sound the drum as if for execution. Suddenly, two colorful dragons appeared and pushed the boat back to float. The official then pardoned Heng's crime. They took Heng northward. After about thirty miles, they reached a good harbor or landing place, where there were tens of thousands of displaced people. Heng stealthily went ashore. The camp was full of dogs that wanted to bite him. Heng was greatly afraid. He saw a lecture hall to the northwest, with many monks inside, and heard the sound of chanting scriptures. Heng rushed to go there. The hall had twelve steps. Heng started to climb the first step when he saw the deceased teacher Fa Zhu sitting on a trapeze-like chair. Fa Zhu saw Heng and said, "I am your teacher, why have you come?" He then stood up and approached the steps, using his handkerchief to hit Heng's face, saying, "Do not come!" Heng very much wanted to go up, so he took steps to ascend the stairs. Fa Zhu pushed him down again. This happened up to three times and then stopped. Heng saw a well on the ground, about 30 to 40 feet deep, with seamless bricks. Heng thought to himself, "This well is natural." Someone by the well said, "If it's not natural, how could it be a well?" Even though Heng saw Fa Zhu, he still hoped and told Heng, "You can go back, and the dogs won't bite you!" Heng returned to the water's edge, but he did not see the boat he had come in. Heng was thirsty and wanted to drink water, so he fell into the water and thus revived. He then became a monk, observed precepts, and ate vegetables. He thought deeply day and night, practicing the highest virtues. The monk Bhikshu Fa Qiao, who was Heng's disciple, is mentioned in the Zhu Lin (Seven). [15]

    晋安罗江县，有霍山，其高蔽日。上有石杵，高僧传石杵作石盂疑杵是杅字之讹面径数丈。杵中泉水，深五六尺，经常流溢。古老传云，列仙之所游饵也。有沙门释僧群，隐居其山，常饮此水，遂以不饥，因而绝粒。晋安太守陶夏，高僧传云陶夔闻而求之。群以水遗陶，出山辄臭。陶于是越海造山。于时天景澄朗。陶践山足，便风雨晦暝。如此者三，竟不得至。群所栖营，与泉隔一涧。旦夕往还，以一木为梁。后旦将渡，辄见一折翅鸭，舒翼当梁，头逆唼；僧群永不得过。欲举锡拨之，恐其坠死。于此绝水，俄而饥卒。时传云，年百四十。群之将死，为众说云：“年少时尝打折一鸭翅，将或此鸭因缘之报乎？”珠林六十三 [16]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [16]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 絕粒 - Practice of fasting, common in various religious traditions, including Buddhism.
    • 因緣之報乎 - Concept of karma or retribution in Buddhism, suggesting that the monk's past actions have led to his current situation.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 高僧傳 - Historical record or biography of eminent monks, indicating the cultural and religious context.
    • 列仙 - Refers to immortals or deities in Chinese mythology, suggesting a spiritual or mystical setting.

    Proper Nouns

    • 晉安羅江縣 - Specific location in ancient China, providing a geographical context.
    • 霍山 - Specific mountain in the region, possibly with local significance.
    • 陶夏 - Historical figure, possibly a local official or ruler, who sought the monk.
    • 珠林六十三 - Specific chapter or section in a historical or literary work, indicating the source of the story.

    Jin'an Luojiang County has a Huo Mountain, whose height blocks the sun. On the mountain, there is a stone basin (or possibly a stone pestle) with a diameter of several zhang. The water in the basin overflows and flows continuously. According to ancient legends, this place is where immortals would play or fish. There was a monk named Sengqun who lived in seclusion on this mountain and drank the water from the basin, which prevented him from feeling hungry, leading him to abstain from food. The governor of Jin'an, Tao Xia (or possibly Tao Kui), heard about Sengqun and sought him out. Sengqun gave Tao the water, but it turned foul as soon as it left the mountain. Tao then crossed the sea to find Sengqun. At the time, the sky was clear and bright. When Tao stepped onto the base of the mountain, it began to rain heavily and become dark. This happened three times, but Tao never reached Sengqun. Sengqun's dwelling was separated from the basin by a stream. He would cross back and forth using a wooden bridge. One morning, as he was about to cross, he saw a duck with a broken wing spreading its wings across the bridge, biting against the wood. Sengqun could never cross the stream after this. He wanted to use his staff to push the duck away but was afraid it would fall and die. Without water, he soon starved to death. It is said that he lived to be 140 years old. Before his death, Sengqun told the crowd, "When I was young, I broke a duck's wing. Could this be the retribution for that deed?" (From Zhu Lin 63) [16]

    晋沙门耆域者，天竺人也。自西域浮海而来，将游关洛，达旧襄阳，欲寄载船北渡。船人见梵沙门衣服弊陋，轻而不载。比船达北岸，耆域亦上。举船皆惊。域前行，有两虎迎之，弭耳掉尾，域手摩其头，虎便入草。于是南北岸奔往请问，域日无所应答。及去，有数百人追之；见域徐行，而众走犹不及。惠帝末，域至洛阳。洛阳道士悉往礼焉。域不为起。译语讥其服章曰，“汝曹分流佛法，不以真诚，但为浮华，求供养耳。”见洛阳宫，曰，“忉利天宫，仿彿似此。 [17]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [17]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 晋沙门耆域者，天竺人也 - Formal introduction of a person, meaning 'The monk Qiyu from Jin is a person from Tianzhu (India).'
    • 浮海而来 - Literally 'crossing the sea to come,' meaning to travel by sea.
    • 弭耳掉尾 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'lowering its ears and wagging its tail,' indicating a friendly or submissive gesture.
    • 无所应答 - Means 'without answering,' indicating silence or refusal to respond.
    • 徐行 - Means 'walking slowly,' often used to describe a deliberate or unhurried pace.
    • 仿彿似此 - Means 'similar to this,' suggesting a resemblance or approximation.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋 - Refers to the Jin dynasty, a period in Chinese history.
    • 沙门 - Refers to a Buddhist monk.
    • 天竺 - The ancient Chinese name for India, the birthplace of Buddhism.
    • 惠帝末 - Refers to the late reign of Emperor Huai of Jin, a period in the Western Jin dynasty.
    • 洛阳道士 - Refers to Taoist practitioners in Luoyang, a significant city in Chinese history.
    • 忉利天宫 - Refers to the Tushita Heaven, a heavenly realm in Buddhist cosmology.

    Proper Nouns

    • 晋 - The Jin dynasty.
    • 耆域 - The name of the monk.
    • 天竺 - The ancient name for India.
    • 关洛 - The regions of Guanzhong and Luoyang.
    • 旧襄阳 - The ancient city of Xiangyang.
    • 惠帝 - Emperor Huai of Jin.
    • 洛阳 - The city of Luoyang.
    • 忉利天宫 - The Tushita Heaven in Buddhist cosmology.

    The monk Qiyu from Jin was a person from Tianzhu (India). He traveled by sea from the Western Regions, intending to visit the regions of Guanzhong and Luoyang, reaching the ancient city of Xiangyang, where he sought to board a ship to cross the river northward. The ship owner, seeing the Buddhist monk in tattered and shabby clothes, dismissed him lightly and did not allow him to board. When the ship finally reached the northern bank, Qiyu also climbed aboard. Everyone on the ship was astonished. As Qiyu walked ahead, two tigers greeted him, lowering their ears and wagging their tails. Qiyu gently patted their heads, and the tigers retreated into the grass. People from both banks then rushed to ask questions, but Qiyu remained silent. When he left, hundreds of people chased after him; they saw Qiyu walking slowly, and despite their efforts, they could not catch up. By the end of Emperor Huai's reign, Qiyu arrived in Luoyang. Taoist practitioners from Luoyang all came to greet him, but Qiyu did not stand up. An interpreter criticized his attire, saying, "You all divide the teachings of the Buddha, not out of sincerity, but merely for show, seeking offerings." When he saw the palace in Luoyang, he said, "The Tushita Heaven is somewhat similar to this." [17]

    当以道力成就，而生死力为之，不亦勤苦乎！”沙门支法渊竺法兴。并年少，后至。域为起立。法渊作礼讫，域以手摩其头曰：“好菩萨，羊中来。”见法兴入门，域大欣笑，往迎作礼。捉法兴手，举着头上曰：“好菩萨，从天人中来？”尚方中有一人，废病数年，垂死。域往视之，谓曰：“何以堕落，生此忧苦？”下病人于地，卧单席上，以应器置腹上，纻布覆之。梵○三偈讫，为梵祝可数千语。寻有臭气满屋。病人曰，活矣。域令人举布，见应器中如污泥者。病人遂瘥。 [18]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [18]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 當以道力成就 - Literal meaning 'should be accomplished through the power of the Dao'; suggests that spiritual or moral cultivation should be achieved through the principles of the Dao.
    • 不亦勤苦乎 - Rhetorical question meaning 'is it not hard work?' or 'is it not laborious?'
    • 好菩萨，羊中来 - Ambiguous phrase; could mean 'good bodhisattva, come from the sheep' or 'good bodhisattva, born from the sheep'; likely a metaphor or reference to a specific Buddhist concept.
    • 好菩萨，从天人中来 - Another ambiguous phrase; could mean 'good bodhisattva, come from the heavenly beings' or 'good bodhisattva, born from the heavenly beings'.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 沙门 - Refers to Buddhist monks or practitioners.
    • 支法渊 - Name of a Buddhist monk.
    • 竺法兴 - Name of a Buddhist monk.
    • 尚方 - Refers to the palace or imperial court, indicating a setting of high status or authority.
    • 梵○三偈 - Refers to Buddhist chants or hymns, indicating a religious or spiritual practice.
    • 梵祝 - Refers to Buddhist chants or prayers, emphasizing the religious context.

    Proper Nouns

    • 域 - Name of a character, likely a high-ranking official or monk.
    • 支法渊 - Name of a Buddhist monk.
    • 竺法兴 - Name of a Buddhist monk.
    • 尚方 - Place name, referring to the palace or imperial court.

    "Should spiritual cultivation be achieved through the power of the Dao, yet it is the force of life and death that accomplishes it, is it not hard work?" The monks Zhi Fayuan and Zhu Faxing, both young and arriving later, stood up for them. Fayuan performed the bow, and Yu rubbed his head and said, "Good bodhisattva, come from the sheep." Seeing Faxing enter, Yu was greatly delighted and smiled, went to welcome him and performed the bow. Taking Faxing's hand, he raised it to his head and said, "Good bodhisattva, come from the heavenly beings?" In the palace, there was a person who had been ill for many years and was on the verge of death. Yu went to see him and said, "Why did you fall into this state, causing such sorrow and suffering?" He lay the patient on the ground, on a single mat, placed a bowl on his abdomen, and covered him with coarse cloth. After reciting three verses of a Buddhist chant, he recited a chant of several thousand words. Soon there was a foul smell filling the room. The patient said, "I am alive." Yu ordered someone to lift the cloth, and saw that the bowl contained something like mud. The patient then recovered. [18]

    长沙太守滕永文，先颇精进。时在洛阳，两脚风挛经年。域为祝，应时得申，数日起行。满水寺中有思惟树，先枯死，域向之祝，旬日，树还生茂。时寺中有竺法行善谈论，时以比乐令。见域，稽首曰：“已见得道证，愿当秉法。”域曰：“守口摄意，身莫犯如是行者，度世去。”法行曰：“得道者当授所未闻。斯言，人岁沙弥亦以之诵，非所望于得道者。”域笑曰：“如子之言，八岁而致诵，百岁不能行。人皆知敬得道者，不知行之即自得。以我观之易耳。妙当在君，岂愠未闻。 [19]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [19]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 先颇精进 - He was previously quite diligent in his spiritual practice.
    • 两脚风挛经年 - His legs were paralyzed by wind evil for many years. This is a medical term describing a condition.
    • 应时得申 - He was able to move his legs at the right time.
    • 数日起行 - After several days, he was able to walk again.
    • 域为祝 - Domain (or Yu) made a wish or prayer.
    • 树还生茂 - The tree grew lush again.
    • 守口摄意 - Guard your speech and control your mind.
    • 身莫犯如是行者 - Do not violate such conduct with your body.
    • 度世去 - Cross over to the other shore.
    • 得道者当授所未闻 - One who has attained the Way should teach what has not been heard before.
    • 如子之言 - As you say.
    • 八岁而致诵 - At eight years old, one can memorize.
    • 百岁不能行 - But in a hundred years, one cannot practice.
    • 人皆知敬得道者 - Everyone knows to respect one who has attained the Way.
    • 不知行之即自得 - But they do not know that by practicing, one attains it oneself.
    • 以我观之易耳 - In my view, it is easy.
    • 妙当在君 - The subtlety lies in you.
    • 岂愠未闻 - Why be angry about not having heard it?

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 长沙 - Changsha, a city in Hunan Province.
    • 太守 - Governor, an administrative title.
    • 滕永文 - Teng Yongwen, a historical figure.
    • 满水寺 - Man Shui Temple, a specific temple.
    • 思惟树 - Tree of thought or tree of contemplation, a symbolic tree in Buddhist contexts.
    • 竺法行 - Zhufa Xing, a monk.
    • 乐令 - Officer in charge of music, a government official.

    Proper Nouns

    • 滕永文 - Teng Yongwen
    • 满水寺 - Man Shui Temple
    • 思惟树 - Tree of thought or tree of contemplation
    • 竺法行 - Zhufa Xing
    • 乐令 - Officer in charge of music

    Teng Yongwen, the governor of Changsha, was previously quite diligent in his spiritual practice. At that time, while in Luoyang, his legs were paralyzed by wind evil for many years. Yu made a wish, and at the right time, he was able to move his legs, and after several days, he was able to walk again. There was a Tree of Contemplation in Man Shui Temple, which had previously died, but Yu made a wish to it, and after ten days, the tree grew lush again. At that time, there was a monk named Zhufa Xing who was good at discussion, and he was compared to the officer in charge of music. When he saw Yu, he bowed respectfully and said, "I have seen the evidence of your attainment of the Way. I wish to uphold the Dharma." Yu replied, "Guard your speech and control your mind, and do not violate such conduct with your body, and cross over to the other shore." Zhufa Xing said, "One who has attained the Way should teach what has not been heard before. Such words, even an eight-year-old novice can recite them, but they are not what one expects from one who has attained the Way." Yu smiled and said, "As you say, at eight years old, one can memorize, but in a hundred years, one cannot practice. Everyone knows to respect one who has attained the Way, but they do not know that by practicing, one attains it oneself. In my view, it is easy. The subtlety lies in you. Why be angry about not having heard it?" [19]

    ”京师贵贱，赠遗衣物，以数千亿万，悉受之。临去，封而留之，唯作旛八百枚，以骆驼负之先遣。随估客西归天竺。又持法兴一纳袈裟随身。谓法兴曰：“此地方大为造新之罪，可哀如何？”域发，送者数千人。于洛阳寺中中食讫，取道。人有其日发长安来，见域在长安寺中。又域所遣估客及骆驼奴达炖煌河上，逢估客弟于天竺来，云近炖煌寺中见域。弟子漯登者云，于流沙北逢域，言语款曲，计其旬日，又域发洛阳时也。而其所行盖已万里矣。珠林二十八高僧传载在耆域之○当迻正 [20]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [20]

    成语表达

    • 京师贵贱 - 指京城中的富贵贫贱，表示广泛的社会阶层。
    • 赠遗衣物 - 意味着赠送衣物作为礼物。
    • 悉受之 - 意味着完全接受。
    • 封而留之 - 意味着封存并留下。
    • 唯作旛八百枚 - 意味着只制作八百面旗帜。
    • 以骆驼负之先遣 - 意味着用骆驼运送并先行出发。
    • 随估客西归天竺 - 意味着跟随商人西行返回印度。
    • 又持法兴一纳袈裟随身 - 意味着携带法兴的一件袈裟随身。
    • 此地方大为造新之罪 - 意味着这个地方因建造新事物而犯下了大罪。
    • 可哀如何 - 意味着多么可悲。
    • 送者数千人 - 意味着数千人送别。
    • 于洛阳寺中中食讫 - 意味着在洛阳寺中吃完饭。
    • 取道 - 意味着选择道路。
    • 人有其日发长安来 - 意味着有人在同一天从长安出发。
    • 见域在长安寺中 - 意味着见到域在长安寺中。
    • 又域所遣估客及骆驼奴达炖煌河上 - 意味着域派遣的商人和骆驼奴到达了敦煌河上。
    • 逢估客弟于天竺来 - 意味着遇到了从印度来的商人的弟弟。
    • 云近炖煌寺中见域 - 意味着说最近在敦煌寺中见到了域。
    • 弟子漯登者云 - 意味着弟子漯登说。
    • 于流沙北逢域 - 意味着在流沙以北遇到了域。
    • 言语款曲 - 意味着详细而诚恳地交谈。
    • 计其旬日 - 意味着计算大约十天。
    • 又域发洛阳时也 - 意味着正是域离开洛阳的时候。
    • 而其所行盖已万里矣 - 意味着他已经行走了大约一万里的路程。
    • 珠林二十八高僧传载在耆域之○当迻正 - 指的是《珠林》第二十八卷（佛教传记集）中关于耆域的条目需要校正。

    文化与历史典故

    • 京师 - 指首都城市，此处可能指洛阳。
    • 贵贱 - 指富贵贫贱，表示广泛的社会阶层。
    • 赠遗衣物 - 赠送衣物作为礼物，是中国文化中常见的习俗。
    • 洛阳寺 - 指洛阳的一座寺庙。
    • 长安寺 - 指长安的一座寺庙。
    • 炖煌 - 指敦煌，丝绸之路上的重要城市。
    • 天竺 - 指印度，佛教的重要中心。
    • 法兴 - 指僧人法兴。
    • 耆域 - 指耆域，文中提到的历史人物。
    • 珠林 - 指《珠林》，佛教传记集。

    专有名词

    • 京师 - 指首都城市。
    • 洛阳 - 指洛阳城市。
    • 长安 - 指长安城市。
    • 炖煌 - 指敦煌城市。
    • 天竺 - 指印度国家。
    • 法兴 - 指僧人法兴。
    • 耆域 - 指耆域。
    • 珠林 - 指《珠林》。
    • 洛阳寺 - 指洛阳寺庙。
    • 长安寺 - 指长安寺庙。
    • 炖煌寺 - 指敦煌寺庙。
    • 流沙 - 指沙漠。

    京城中的富贵贫贱，赠送衣物作为礼物，数量达到数千亿，全部接受。临行前，将衣物封存并留下，只制作了八百面旗帜，用骆驼运送并先行出发。跟随商人西行返回印度。又携带法兴的一件袈裟随身。对法兴说：“这个地方因建造新事物而犯下了大罪，多么可悲啊！”域离开时，送别的人有数千人。在洛阳寺中吃完饭后，选择道路出发。有人在同一天从长安出发，见到域在长安寺中。域派遣的商人和骆驼奴到达了敦煌河上，遇到了从印度来的商人的弟弟，说最近在敦煌寺中见到了域。弟子漯登说，在流沙以北遇到了域，详细而诚恳地交谈，计算大约十天，正是域离开洛阳的时候。而他已经行走了大约一万里的路程。《珠林》第二十八卷的高僧传记载了关于耆域的条目，应当进行校正。 [20]

    晋沙门佛调，不知何国人。往来常山，积年业，尚纯朴，不表辞饰；时咸以此重之。常山有奉法者兄弟二人，居去寺百里。兄妇病甚笃，载出寺侧，以近医药。兄既奉调为师，朝昼常在寺中，咨询行道。异日。调忽往其家，弟具问嫂所苦，并审兄安否。调曰：“病者粗可，卿兄如常。”调去后，弟亦策马继往，言及调旦来。兄惊曰：“和尚旦初不出寺，汝何容相见？”兄弟争问调，调笑而不答，咸共异焉。调或独入深山，一年半岁，赍干饭数升，还恒有余。有人尝随调山行数十里。天?大雪，调入石穴虎窟中宿。 [21]

    A monk named Buddha Diao from the Jin Dynasty, of unknown nationality, frequently traveled to Changshan, where he accumulated years of experience. He maintained a simple and unadorned demeanor, not using elaborate language or embellishments. At the time, everyone held him in high regard for this.

    In Changshan, there were two brothers who followed the Dharma. Their home was about a hundred li away from the temple. One day, the brother's wife became seriously ill and was taken to the temple side for medical treatment. The brother, who had Buddha Diao as his teacher, usually stayed in the temple day and night, seeking guidance on the path of practice. One day, Buddha Diao suddenly visited their home. The younger brother asked about his sister-in-law's condition and inquired about his brother's health. Buddha Diao replied, "Her condition is somewhat better, and your brother is as usual." After Buddha Diao left, the younger brother also rode a horse to follow him and mentioned that the monk had come early in the morning. The elder brother was surprised and said, "The monk did not leave the temple early in the morning. How could you have seen him?" The brothers argued about the monk, but he only smiled and did not answer, leaving them all puzzled.

    Buddha Diao sometimes traveled alone into deep mountains, staying there for a year or half a year. He carried a few measures of cooked rice and always had some left over when he returned. Once, a person followed Buddha Diao on a mountain journey for dozens of li. It snowed heavily, and Buddha Diao slept in a tiger's den. [21]

    虎还横卧窟前。调语曰：“我夺汝居处，有愧如何！”虎弭耳下山。随者骇惧。调自克亡期，远近悉至。乃与诀曰：“天地长久，尚有崩坏；岂况人物，而欲永存？若能荡除三垢，专心真净；形数虽乖，而神会必同。”众咸涕流。调还房端坐，以衣蒙头，奄然而终。终后数年，调白衣弟子八人，入西山伐木，忽见调在高岩上，衣服鲜明，姿仪畅悦。皆惊喜作礼问，“和尚尚在此耶？”答曰，“吾常自在耳。”具问知故消息，良久乃去。八人便舍事还家，向同法者说，众无以验之。共发冢开棺，不见其尸。珠林二十八 [22]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [22]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 虎还横卧窟前 - Literal description of a tiger returning and lying down in front of its den.
    • 我夺汝居处，有愧如何 - Rhetorical question expressing guilt or regret for taking the tiger's place.
    • 虎弭耳下山 - Describes the tiger lowering its ears and descending the mountain, indicating submission or acceptance.
    • 调自克亡期 - Means 'the monk predicted his own time of death.'
    • 天地长久，尚有崩坏 - Philosophical statement about the impermanence of all things, including the heavens and earth.
    • 岂况人物，而欲永存 - Rhetorical question emphasizing the inevitability of death for humans.
    • 若能荡除三垢，专心真净 - Means 'if one can cleanse oneself of the three defilements and focus on true purity.'
    • 形数虽乖，而神会必同 - Means 'even if physical forms differ, the spiritual essence will surely be the same.'
    • 调白衣弟子八人，入西山伐木 - Describes eight lay disciples of the monk entering the west mountain to cut wood.
    • 忽见调在高岩上，衣服鲜明，姿仪畅悦 - Describes the sudden appearance of the monk on a high cliff, dressed brightly and looking joyful.
    • 皆惊喜作礼问 - Means 'all were surprised and joyful, bowing to ask.'
    • 吾常自在耳 - Means 'I have always been here.'
    • 具问知故消息 - Means 'they asked about the news of the old friends.'
    • 调还房端坐，以衣蒙头，奄然而终 - Describes the monk returning to his room, sitting upright, covering his head with a cloth, and dying suddenly.
    • 终后数年，调白衣弟子八人，入西山伐木 - Repeats the description of the eight lay disciples entering the west mountain to cut wood.
    • 忽见调在高岩上，衣服鲜明，姿仪畅悦 - Repeats the description of the monk's sudden appearance.
    • 皆惊喜作礼问，和尚尚在此耶 - Repeats the disciples' reaction and question.
    • 吾常自在耳 - Repeats the monk's response.
    • 具问知故消息，良久乃去 - Repeats the disciples asking about the news of the old friends and then leaving.
    • 八人便舍事还家，向同法者说 - Describes the eight disciples giving up their work and returning home to tell their fellow practitioners.
    • 众无以验之 - Means 'the others had no way to verify this.'
    • 共发冢开棺，不见其尸 - Describes the others opening the tomb and finding no body.
    • 珠林二十八 - Refers to the source text, likely a chapter or section in a collection of stories or anecdotes.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 虎 - Refers to a tiger, a common symbol in Chinese culture, often representing strength and ferocity.
    • 调 - Refers to a Buddhist monk, likely a respected figure in the community.
    • 白衣弟子 - Refers to lay disciples, individuals who follow the teachings of a monk but are not ordained.
    • 西山 - Refers to the west mountain, a specific location in the narrative.
    • 三垢 - Refers to the three defilements, which are typically understood as greed, hatred, and delusion in Buddhist philosophy.
    • 真净 - Refers to true purity, a state of mind free from defilements.
    • 珠林二十八 - Likely refers to a specific chapter or section in a collection of Buddhist stories or anecdotes, possibly from a text like the 'Buddhist Treasury' (珠林).

    Proper Nouns

    • 调 - The name of the monk, a key figure in the narrative.
    • 珠林二十八 - The title of the source text, likely a chapter or section in a collection of Buddhist stories or anecdotes.

    The tiger returned and lay down in front of its den. The monk said, "I have taken your dwelling, how can I not feel ashamed!" The tiger lowered its ears and descended the mountain. Those who followed were filled with fear. The monk had predicted his own time of death, and people from far and wide came to bid him farewell. He said to them, "The heavens and earth endure for a long time, yet they still have their moments of collapse; how much more so for human beings, who desire eternal existence? If one can cleanse oneself of the three defilements and focus on true purity, even if physical forms differ, the spiritual essence will surely be the same." All present shed tears. The monk returned to his room, sat upright, covered his head with a cloth, and died suddenly. Several years later, eight of the monk's lay disciples entered the west mountain to cut wood. Suddenly, they saw the monk on a high cliff, dressed brightly and looking joyful. They were all surprised and joyful, bowing to ask, "Is the master still here?" He replied, "I have always been here." They asked about the news of the old friends, and after a long while, they departed. The eight disciples then gave up their work and returned home to tell their fellow practitioners. The others had no way to verify this. They opened the tomb and found no body. From the "Buddhist Treasury," Chapter Twenty-Eight. [22]

    晋犍陀勒，不知何国人也。尝游洛邑，周历数年。虽敬其风操，而莫能测焉。后语人曰，盘鸱高僧传鸱作?山中有古塔寺，若能修建，其福无量。众人许之。与俱入山。既至，唯草木深芜，莫知基朕。勒指示曰：“此是寺基也。”众试掘之，果得塔下石础。复示讲堂，僧房，井灶。开凿寻求，皆如其言。于是始疑其异。寺既修，勒为僧主。去洛百里。每朝至洛邑，赴会听讲竟，辄乞油一钵，擎之还寺。虽复去来早晚，未曾失中晡之期。有人日能行数百里者，欲随而验之，乃与俱。此人驰而不及；勒顾笑曰：“汝执吾袈裟，可以不倦。”既持衣后，不及移晷，便已至寺。其人休息数日乃还。方悟神人。后不知终。珠林八十二 [23]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [23]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 不知何国人也 - Classical expression meaning 'does not know what country he is from.'
    • 若能修建，其福无量 - Means 'if it can be restored, the merit will be boundless.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋 - Refers to the Jin Dynasty (266-420 CE).
    • 洛邑 - Refers to Luoyang, an important city in Chinese history, particularly during the Jin Dynasty.
    • 高僧传 - A title of a text, likely referring to a biography or collection of stories about eminent monks.

    Proper Nouns

    • 犍陀勒 - A proper name, likely a Buddhist monk or traveler from the Jin Dynasty.
    • 珠林八十二 - A reference to a specific chapter or section in a text, likely a collection of Buddhist stories or legends.

    Jin Dynasty's Kandala, whose nationality is unknown, once traveled to Luoyang and spent several years there. Although people respected his manner and conduct, no one could fully understand him. Later, he told others that in a mountain there was an ancient pagoda temple, and if it could be restored, the merit would be boundless. Everyone agreed to this proposal. He went with them into the mountains. Upon arrival, they found only deep and overgrown vegetation, and no one knew where the base of the temple was. Kandala pointed out and said, "This is the site of the temple." They tried digging and indeed found the stone base of the stupa. He then showed the lecture hall, monks' quarters, and wells and kitchens. When they excavated and searched, everything was as he had said. Thus, they began to suspect his extraordinary nature. After the temple was restored, Kandala became the abbot. It was a hundred miles away from Luoyang. Every morning he would go to Luoyang, after attending gatherings and listening to lectures, he would then ask for a bowl of oil and carry it back to the temple. Although he went and came at different times, he never missed the noon hour. There was someone who could travel hundreds of miles in a day, who wanted to follow and verify it, so he went with him. This person ran but could not catch up; Kandala looked back and smiled, saying, "You hold my robe, and you will not get tired." After holding the robe, before the shadow moved, he had already arrived at the temple. This person rested for several days and then returned. Only then did they realize he was a divine being. Later, it is unknown what became of him. Chapter 82 of Zhu Lin. [23]

    晋抵世常，中山人也。家道殷富。太康中，禁晋人作沙门。世常奉法精进，潜于宅中起立精舍，供养沙门；于法兰亦在焉。僧众来者，无所辞却。有一比丘，姿形顽陋，衣服尘敝，跋涉涂泞，来造世常。常出为作礼，命奴取水，为其洗足。比丘曰：“世常应自洗我足。”常曰：“年老疲瘵，以奴自代。”比丘不听。世常窃骂而去比丘。便见神足变身八尺，颜容瑰伟，飞行而去。世常抚膺悔叹，自扑泥中。时抵家僧尼及行路者五六十人，俱得望视，见在空中数十丈上，了了分明。奇芬异气，经月不歇。法兰即名理法师见宗者也。有记在后卷传。兰以语于弟子法阶，阶每说之，道俗多闻。珠林二十八 [24]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [24]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 奉法精进 - Devoted to the Dharma and making diligent efforts.
    • 神足变身 - Supernatural powers or abilities; transformation into a figure eight feet tall with a magnificent appearance.
    • 抚膺悔叹 - Gesture of regret and sorrow.
    • 奇芬异气 - Unusual and mystical phenomena.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋 - Refers to the state of Jin during the Three Kingdoms period.
    • 太康 - Reign title of Emperor Tai Kang of the Western Jin dynasty.
    • 沙门 - Refers to Buddhist monks.
    • 精舍 - Refers to a monastery or place of worship.
    • 比丘 - Refers to a male Buddhist monk.
    • 名理法师见宗者 - Refers to a famous monk known for his wisdom and insight.

    Proper Nouns

    • 晋抵世常 - The name of the subject.
    • 中山 - The state from which the subject is from.
    • 法兰 - The name of the monk Falei.
    • 法阶 - The name of Falei's disciple.
    • 珠林二十八 - The source or reference of the story.

    Shi Chang of Jin, a man from the state of Zhongshan, was a man of great wealth. During the reign of Emperor Tai Kang, it was forbidden for people of Jin to become monks. Shi Chang, however, was devoted to the Dharma and made diligent efforts. He secretly built a monastery in his home to support and provide for monks, and there was also the monk Falei among them. No matter who came, he did not refuse them. One day, a monk with an ugly and unattractive appearance, wearing dusty and worn-out clothes, traveled through muddy roads to visit Shi Chang. Shi Chang came out to greet him with respect and ordered his servant to fetch water to wash the monk's feet. The monk said, "Shi Chang should wash my feet himself." Shi Chang replied, "I am old and weak, so I have my servant do it." The monk would not listen. Shi Chang secretly cursed the monk and left. The monk then transformed into a figure eight feet tall, with a magnificent appearance, and flew away. Shi Chang beat his chest in regret and fell into the mud. At that time, about fifty or sixty monks, nuns, and passersby were all able to see the monk flying high in the sky, clearly visible. A strange fragrance and unusual aura lasted for months. Falei was a famous monk known as the Seer of the Dharma. There is a record of this in the subsequent volume. Falei told this to his disciple Fahjie, who often recounted it, and many people, both monastic and lay, heard it. This story is recorded in the Zhu Lin (Jewel Forest), volume twenty-eight. [24]

    晋沙门康法朗，学于中山。永嘉中，与四比丘西入天竺。行过流沙，千有余里。见道边败坏佛图，无复堂殿，蓬蒿没人。法朗等下拜瞻礼，见有二僧，各居其一：一人读经，一人患痢。秽污盈房。其读经者了不营视，朗等恻然兴念，留为煮粥，埽除浣濯。至六日，病者稍困，注痢如泉。朗等共料理之。其夜，朗等并谓病者必不移旦。至明晨往视，容色光悦，痛状休然。广记引作病状顿除屋中秽物，皆是华馨。朗等乃悟是得道冥士以试人也。病者曰：“隔房比丘，是我和尚。久得道慧，可往礼觐。”法朗等先嫌读经沙门无慈爱心，闻已乃作礼悔过。读经者曰：“诸君诚契并至，同当入道。朗公宿学业浅，此世未得愿也。”谓朗伴云：“慧此居植根深，当现世得愿。”因而留之。法朗后还中山为大法师，道俗宗之。珠林九十五广记八十九 [25]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [25]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 永嘉中 - Refers to the Yongjia period (307–313 AD) during the Jin dynasty, a time of political turmoil and migration.
    • 西入天竺 - Means 'travel west into India,' where '天竺' is the ancient Chinese name for India.
    • 流沙 - Refers to the vast desert regions, often used in classical texts to describe the Gobi or Taklamakan deserts.
    • 敗壞佛圖 - Means 'ruined stupa' or 'decayed Buddhist structure,' where '佛圖' is an older term for a Buddhist temple or stupa.
    • 蓬蒿没人 - Describes the overgrowth of weeds and grass, indicating neglect or abandonment.
    • 下拜瞻礼 - Means 'prostrated and paid homage,' a formal act of respect.
    • 患痢 - Refers to suffering from dysentery, a severe illness.
    • 秽污盈房 - Means 'filth and excrement filled the room,' describing the severity of the illness.
    • 了不营视 - Means 'showed no concern or care,' indicating indifference.
    • 恻然兴念 - Means 'felt compassion and took action,' describing the monks' response to the suffering.
    • 煮粥，埽除浣濯 - Refers to the act of cooking porridge and cleaning, showing their care for the sick monk.
    • 容色光悦 - Means 'face radiant and joyful,' indicating a sudden improvement in health.
    • 痛状休然 - Means 'the pain vanished,' describing the disappearance of the illness.
    • 华馨 - Means 'fragrant flowers,' symbolizing purity and transformation.
    • 得道冥士 - Refers to a monk who has attained enlightenment but remains hidden, testing others.
    • 隔房比丘 - Means 'the monk in the adjacent room,' referring to the enlightened monk.
    • 久得道慧 - Means 'has long attained wisdom and enlightenment.'
    • 朗公宿学业浅 - Means 'Master Lang's accumulated learning is shallow,' indicating that he has not yet reached the level of enlightenment.
    • 此世未得愿也 - Means 'he will not attain his wish in this life,' suggesting that he will not achieve enlightenment in this lifetime.
    • 慧此居植根深 - Means 'Master Hui has deeply rooted himself in this place,' indicating that he is firmly established in his spiritual practice.
    • 当现世得愿 - Means 'will attain his wish in this life,' suggesting that Master Hui will achieve enlightenment in this lifetime.
    • 因而留之 - Means 'therefore, he was left behind,' indicating that Master Hui was allowed to stay in the place.
    • 大法师 - Refers to a senior monk or teacher of Buddhism.
    • 道俗宗之 - Means 'both monks and laypeople revered him,' indicating his high status and respect.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋 - Refers to the Jin dynasty (266–420 AD), a period of Chinese history marked by political fragmentation and cultural flourishing.
    • 沙门 - Refers to Buddhist monks or monastics.
    • 永嘉中 - The Yongjia period was a time of significant political and social upheaval, including the invasion of northern nomadic tribes and the migration of the population southward.
    • 天竺 - The ancient Chinese name for India, where Buddhism originated.
    • 佛图 - An older term for a Buddhist temple or stupa, reflecting the early transmission of Buddhism to China.
    • 流沙 - Refers to the vast desert regions, often used in classical texts to describe the journey to India.
    • 得道冥士 - Refers to a monk who has attained enlightenment but remains hidden, testing others' faith and compassion.
    • 大法师 - A senior monk or teacher of Buddhism, often holding a position of authority and respect.
    • 道俗宗之 - Indicates the high regard and respect given to Master Lang by both monks and laypeople, reflecting his status as a respected teacher and leader.

    Proper Nouns

    • 康法朗 - The name of the monk, who is the main character of the story.
    • 中山 - A place name, likely referring to a region in northern China.
    • 天竺 - The ancient Chinese name for India.
    • 流沙 - A term referring to the vast desert regions.
    • 佛图 - An older term for a Buddhist temple or stupa.
    • 朗公 - A respectful title for Master Lang, indicating his status as a senior monk.
    • 慧 - The name of another monk, who is also a central figure in the story.

    During the Jin dynasty, a monk named Kang Falang studied in Zhongshan. In the Yongjia period, he traveled west with four other monks to India. They crossed the vast desert, covering more than a thousand miles. Along the way, they saw a ruined stupa by the roadside, with no remaining halls or pavilions, overgrown with weeds and grass. Kang Falang and his companions prostrated and paid homage, and they saw two monks, each in their own room: one was reading scriptures, and the other was suffering from dysentery. The room was filled with filth and excrement. The monk reading the scriptures showed no concern, but Kang Falang and his companions felt compassion and took action, staying to cook porridge and clean the room. After six days, the sick monk became increasingly weak, and his dysentery was as severe as a gushing spring. Kang Falang and his companions cared for him together. That night, they all believed the sick monk would not survive until morning. But the next morning, when they went to check on him, his face was radiant and joyful, and the pain had vanished. The house was now filled with the fragrance of flowers. Kang Falang and his companions realized that the sick monk was an enlightened being testing them. The sick monk said, "The monk in the adjacent room is my master. He has long attained wisdom and enlightenment. You should go and pay your respects." Kang Falang and his companions had previously disapproved of the monk reading scriptures for lacking compassion, but upon hearing this, they prostrated and repented. The monk reading the scriptures said, "All of you have come together with sincere faith, and you are all destined to enter the path. Master Lang's accumulated learning is shallow, and he will not attain his wish in this life." He then told Kang Falang's companion, "Master Hui has deeply rooted himself in this place and will attain his wish in this life." Therefore, he was left behind. After this, Kang Falang returned to Zhongshan and became a great master of Buddhism, revered by both monks and laypeople. [25]

    晋竺长舒者，其先西域人也。世有资货，为富人。竺居晋元康中内徙洛阳。长舒奉法精至；尤好诵观世音经。其后邻比失火。长舒家悉草屋，又正下风，自计火已逼近，政复出物，所全无几，乃敕家人不得辇物。亦无灌救者。唯至心诵经。有顷，火烧其邻屋，与长舒隔篱，而风忽自回，火亦际屋而止。于时咸以为灵。里中有轻险少年四五人，共毁笑之，云风偶自转，此复何神。伺时燥夕，当爇其屋：能令不然者，可也。其后天甚早燥。风起亦驶，少年辈密共束炬掷其屋上。三掷三灭，乃大惊惧，各走还家。明晨，相率诣长舒自说昨事，稽颡辞谢。长舒答曰：“我了无神，政诵念观世音，当是威灵所祐。诸君但当洗心信向耳。”自是邻里乡党咸敬异焉。珠林二十三 [26]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [26]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 其先西域人也 - Means 'his ancestors were from the Western Regions,' referring to people from Central Asia or beyond.
    • 世有資貨，為富人 - Means 'he was a wealthy person by inheritance,' indicating long-standing wealth.
    • 居晉元康中內徙洛陽 - Means 'he moved to Luoyang during the Yuan Kang period of the Jin Dynasty,' specifying the time and place.
    • 長舒奉法精至 - Means 'Zhang Shu was devout and meticulous in his observance of the law,' emphasizing his religious devotion.
    • 尤好诵觀世音經 - Means 'he especially loved reciting the *Guanyin Sutra*,' highlighting his specific religious practice.
    • 自計火已逼近 - Means 'he thought the fire was already close,' indicating a sense of urgency.
    • 政復出物，所全無幾 - Means 'even if he took out the goods, he could save very little,' suggesting futility.
    • 乃敕家人不得辇物 - Means 'he then ordered his family not to move the goods,' indicating a decision to protect something else.
    • 亦無灌救者 - Means 'no one else was trying to extinguish the fire,' emphasizing his isolation.
    • 有頃，火烧其邻屋，與長舒隔篱，而風忽自回，火亦際屋而止 - Means 'after a while, the fire burned the neighboring house, which was separated from Zhang Shu's by a fence, but the wind suddenly changed direction, and the fire stopped at the edge of the house,' describing a miraculous event.
    • 里中有輕險少年四五人，共毀笑之 - Means 'there were a few reckless young men in the neighborhood who mocked him,' indicating social skepticism.
    • 伺時燥夕，當爇其屋 - Means 'they waited for a dry evening to burn his house,' showing their malicious intent.
    • 三擲三滅，乃大驚惧 - Means 'they threw three times and each time the fire was extinguished, so they were greatly frightened,' describing the supernatural intervention.
    • 明晨，相率诣長舒自说昨事，稽颡辞谢 - Means 'the next morning, they came together to Zhang Shu and told him about what happened last night, bowing and apologizing,' indicating their repentance.
    • 我了无神，政诵念觀世音，當是威灵所祐 - Means 'I have no divine power; I just recite the *Guanyin Sutra*, and it must be the protective power of the bodhisattva,' explaining his belief.
    • 諸君但当洗心信向耳 - Means 'you all should cleanse your hearts and have faith,' offering a moral lesson.
    • 自是邻里乡党咸敬异焉 - Means 'from then on, neighbors and fellow villagers all respected and admired him,' summarizing the impact of the event.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晉元康中 - Refers to the Yuan Kang period of the Jin Dynasty (291-299 AD).
    • 洛陽 - Refers to Luoyang, the capital of the Jin Dynasty.
    • 觀世音經 - Refers to the *Guanyin Sutra*, a Buddhist text dedicated to the bodhisattva Guanyin.
    • 珠林二十三 - Refers to the 23rd chapter of the *Zhu Lin* (Pearl Forest), a collection of Buddhist stories and anecdotes.

    Proper Nouns

    • 晉 - The Jin Dynasty.
    • 元康 - The Yuan Kang period of the Jin Dynasty.
    • 洛陽 - Luoyang.
    • 觀世音經 - The *Guanyin Sutra*.
    • 珠林二十三 - The 23rd chapter of the *Zhu Lin*.

    Zhang Shu of Jin was a descendant of people from the Western Regions. His family had long been wealthy. During the Yuan Kang period of the Jin Dynasty, he moved to Luoyang. Zhang Shu was devout and meticulous in his observance of the law, especially loving to recite the Guanyin Sutra. Later, a fire broke out in the neighborhood. Zhang Shu's house was made of thatch and was located downwind, so he thought the fire was already close. Even if he took out the goods, he could save very little, so he ordered his family not to move the goods. No one else was trying to extinguish the fire. He only recited the Guanyin Sutra with utmost sincerity. After a while, the fire burned the neighboring house, which was separated from Zhang Shu's by a fence, but the wind suddenly changed direction, and the fire stopped at the edge of the house. At that time, everyone believed it was a miraculous event. There were a few reckless young men in the neighborhood who mocked him, saying the wind just happened to turn, and what kind of divine power was this? They waited for a dry evening to burn his house: if they could prevent the fire from spreading, they would be satisfied. Later, the weather was very dry. The wind also blew strongly, and the young men secretly gathered torches and threw them onto Zhang Shu's roof. They threw three times, and each time the fire was extinguished, so they were greatly frightened and ran back to their homes. The next morning, they came together to Zhang Shu and told him about what happened last night, bowing and apologizing. Zhang Shu replied, "I have no divine power; I just recite the Guanyin Sutra, and it must be the protective power of the bodhisattva. You all should cleanse your hearts and have faith." From then on, neighbors and fellow villagers all respected and admired him. Zhu Lin 23. [26]

    晋浔阳庐山西有龙泉精舍，即慧远沙门之所立也。远始南渡，爱其区丘。欲创寺宇，未知定方。遣诸弟子访履林涧，疲息此地。群僧并渴。率同立誓曰：“若使此处，宜立精舍，当愿神力，即出佳泉。”乃以杖掘地，清泉涌出。遂畜为池。因构堂于其后。天尝亢旱，远率诸僧转海龙王经，为民祈雨。转读未毕，泉中有物，形如巨蛇，腾空而去。俄尔洪雨四澍，高下普沾。以有龙瑞，故名焉。珠林三十三 [27]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [27]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 始南渡 - Began to cross south or first moved south, referring to the historical context of the Eastern Jin dynasty when many people migrated south.
    • 愛其區丘 - Loved its terrain, indicating a preference for a particular landscape.
    • 欲創寺宇 - Wanted to build a temple, expressing the intention to establish a religious institution.
    • 未知定方 - Did not know the exact location, suggesting uncertainty about where to build.
    • 遣諸弟子訪履林涧 - Sent his disciples to explore the woods and streams, describing the act of sending followers to scout the area.
    • 疲息此地 - Tired and rested here, indicating that the disciples found a suitable spot for rest.
    • 率同立誓曰 - Together they took an oath, emphasizing the collective nature of the vow.
    • 若使此处，宜立精舍，当愿神力，即出佳泉 - If this place is suitable for building a hermitage, may the divine power bring forth a good spring, a vow invoking divine assistance.
    • 乃以杖掘地 - Then he dug with a staff, describing the action of digging with a simple tool.
    • 清泉涌出 - A clear spring gushed forth, indicating the miraculous appearance of water.
    • 遂畜为池 - Thus they collected it into a pond, describing the creation of a water source.
    • 因构堂于其后 - And built a hall behind it, indicating the construction of a building.
    • 天尝亢旱 - The sky often suffered from drought, describing a period of severe dryness.
    • 远率诸僧转海龙王经，为民祈雨 - Far led the monks to chant the *Hai Long Wang Jing* (Sea Dragon King Sutra) to pray for rain, describing a religious ritual.
    • 转读未毕 - Before the chanting was completed, indicating the timing of the event.
    • 泉中有物，形如巨蛇 - There was something in the spring, shaped like a giant snake, describing a supernatural occurrence.
    • 腾空而去 - It rose into the air and departed, indicating the snake's departure.
    • 俄尔洪雨四澍 - Soon after, heavy rain fell from all directions, describing the immediate effect of the ritual.
    • 高下普沾 - Raining down on both high and low places, emphasizing the widespread nature of the rain.
    • 以有龙瑞 - Because of the dragon's auspicious sign, explaining the reason for naming the place.
    • 故名焉 - Thus it was named, concluding the story with the naming of the place.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋浔阳庐山西有龙泉精舍 - On the west of Mount Lu in Xunyang, Jin, there is the Longquan Hermitage, providing the geographical and historical context.
    • 慧远沙门 - Master Huiyuan, a famous Buddhist monk and scholar of the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 南渡 - Refers to the migration of people from the north to the south during the Eastern Jin dynasty, a significant historical event.
    • 龙泉精舍 - Longquan Hermitage, a specific location associated with Master Huiyuan.
    • 转海龙王经 - Chanting the *Hai Long Wang Jing* (Sea Dragon King Sutra), a Buddhist text believed to have the power to summon rain.

    Proper Nouns

    • 晋 - Jin, the name of the dynasty.
    • 浔阳庐山 - Xunyang, Mount Lu, the specific location.
    • 龙泉精舍 - Longquan Hermitage, the name of the hermitage.
    • 慧远 - Huiyuan, the name of the Buddhist monk.
    • 海龙王经 - Hai Long Wang Jing (Sea Dragon King Sutra), the name of the Buddhist text.

    On the west of Mount Lu in Xunyang, Jin, there is the Longquan Hermitage, which was established by Master Huiyuan. When Master Huiyuan first moved south, he loved the terrain of this place. He wanted to build a temple but did not know the exact location. He sent his disciples to explore the woods and streams, and they became tired and rested here. All the monks were thirsty. Together they took an oath, saying, "If this place is suitable for building a hermitage, may the divine power bring forth a good spring." Then he dug with a staff, and a clear spring gushed forth. Thus they collected it into a pond and built a hall behind it. During a period of severe drought, Master Huiyuan led the monks to chant the Hai Long Wang Jing (Sea Dragon King Sutra) to pray for rain. Before the chanting was completed, there was something in the spring, shaped like a giant snake, which rose into the air and departed. Soon after, heavy rain fell from all directions, raining down on both high and low places. Because of the dragon's auspicious sign, it was named Longquan. This is recorded in Zhu Lin Thirty-Three. [27]

    晋沙门于法兰，高阳人也。十五而出家。器识沉秀，业操贞整。寺于深岩。尝夜坐禅，虎入其室；因蹲床前。兰以手摩其头。虎奋耳而伏。数日乃去。竺护，炖煌人也。风神情宇，亦兰之次。于时经典新译，梵语数多，辞句烦芜，章偈不整；乃领其旨要，刊其游文。亦养徒山中。山有清涧，汲漱所资。有采薪者，尝秽其水；水即竭涸，俄而绝流。护临涧徘徊，叹曰：“水若遂竭，吾将何资！”言终而清流洋溢，寻复盈涧。并武惠时人也。支道林为之像赞曰：“于氏超世，综体玄旨。嘉遁山泽；仁感虎兕。护公澄寂，道德渊美。微吟空涧，枯泉还水。”法苑珠林六十三 [28]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [28]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 十五而出家 - Common phrase meaning 'at the age of fifteen, he left home to become a monk.'
    • 器识沉秀 - Refers to a person with deep insight and outstanding qualities.
    • 业操贞整 - Means 'conduct is pure and upright.'
    • 寺于深岩 - Literally means 'temple in a deep cave,' indicating a secluded place.
    • 尝夜坐禅 - Means 'he once sat in meditation at night.'
    • 虎入其室 - Means 'a tiger entered his room.'
    • 因蹲床前 - Means 'and then crouched in front of the bed.'
    • 兰以手摩其头 - Means 'Yulan touched its head with his hand.'
    • 虎奋耳而伏 - Means 'the tiger raised its ears and crouched down.'
    • 数日乃去 - Means 'after several days, it left.'
    • 竺护，炖煌人也 - Means 'Zhu Hu, a native of Dunhuang.'
    • 风神情宇 - Refers to a person's demeanor and appearance.
    • 亦兰之次 - Means 'also second only to Yulan.'
    • 经典新译 - Means 'newly translated scriptures.'
    • 梵语数多 - Means 'there are many Sanskrit terms.'
    • 辞句烦芜 - Means 'the phrases are complicated and messy.'
    • 章偈不整 - Means 'the verses are not well-arranged.'
    • 刊其游文 - Means 'to edit out unnecessary words.'
    • 养徒山中 - Means 'to cultivate disciples in the mountains.'
    • 山有清涧 - Means 'the mountain has a clear stream.'
    • 汲漱所资 - Means 'the source for drinking and washing.'
    • 有采薪者，尝秽其水 - Means 'there was someone who cut wood and once polluted the water.'
    • 水即竭涸 - Means 'the water immediately dried up.'
    • 俄而绝流 - Means 'soon after, the stream stopped flowing.'
    • 护临涧徘徊 - Means 'Hu stood by the stream and wandered around.'
    • 叹曰 - Means 'he sighed and said.'
    • 水若遂竭，吾将何资 - Means 'if the water really dries up, what will I use for drinking and washing?'
    • 言终而清流洋溢 - Means 'after he finished speaking, the clear stream flowed abundantly.'
    • 寻复盈涧 - Means 'soon the stream was full again.'
    • 并武惠时人也 - Means 'both were contemporaries of Emperor Wu and Emperor Huizong.'
    • 支道林为之像赞曰 - Means 'Zhi Daolin wrote an inscription for them.'
    • 于氏超世 - Means 'Yulan transcended the world.'
    • 综体玄旨 - Means 'summarized the profound meanings.'
    • 嘉遁山泽 - Means 'retired to the mountains and lakes.'
    • 仁感虎兕 - Means 'his kindness moved the tiger.'
    • 护公澄寂 - Means 'Zhu Hu was calm and serene.'
    • 道德渊美 - Means 'his moral character was profound and beautiful.'
    • 微吟空涧 - Means 'he softly sang in the empty valley.'
    • 枯泉还水 - Means 'the dried-up spring returned to flowing.'
    • 法苑珠林六十三 - The title of the text, meaning 'Jewel Forest of the Dharma Garden, Chapter 63.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋 - Refers to the Jin Dynasty, a period in Chinese history.
    • 沙门 - Refers to Buddhist monks.
    • 于法兰 - A historical figure, Yulan Fan, a Buddhist monk.
    • 高阳人也 - Means 'from Gaoyang,' a place name.
    • 竺护 - A historical figure, Zhu Hu, another Buddhist monk.
    • 炖煌 - Refers to Dunhuang, an important city in the Silk Road.
    • 武惠 - Refers to Emperor Wu and Emperor Huizong, rulers of the Jin Dynasty.
    • 支道林 - A historical figure, Zhi Daolin, a famous Buddhist monk and scholar.
    • 法苑珠林 - A collection of Buddhist stories and teachings.
    • 六十三 - Refers to the specific chapter in the collection.

    Proper Nouns

    • 晋 - The Jin Dynasty.
    • 沙门 - Buddhist monks.
    • 于法兰 - Yulan Fan, a Buddhist monk.
    • 高阳 - Gaoyang, a place name.
    • 竺护 - Zhu Hu, a Buddhist monk.
    • 炖煌 - Dunhuang, a city in the Silk Road.
    • 武惠 - Emperor Wu and Emperor Huizong, rulers of the Jin Dynasty.
    • 支道林 - Zhi Daolin, a famous Buddhist monk and scholar.
    • 法苑珠林 - A collection of Buddhist stories and teachings.
    • 六十三 - Chapter 63 of the collection.

    Yulan Fan, a monk from the Jin Dynasty, was from Gaoyang. At the age of fifteen, he left home to become a monk. He had deep insight and outstanding qualities, and his conduct was pure and upright. His temple was located in a deep cave. Once, he sat in meditation at night, and a tiger entered his room. The tiger then crouched in front of the bed. Yulan touched its head with his hand, and the tiger raised its ears and crouched down. After several days, it left.

    Zhu Hu, a native of Dunhuang, had a demeanor and appearance that were second only to Yulan. At the time, newly translated scriptures were abundant, with many Sanskrit terms. The phrases were complicated and messy, and the verses were not well-arranged. Zhu Hu edited out unnecessary words and cultivated disciples in the mountains. The mountain had a clear stream, which served as the source for drinking and washing. There was someone who cut wood and once polluted the water. The water immediately dried up, and soon after, the stream stopped flowing. Zhu Hu stood by the stream and wandered around, sighing and saying, "If the water really dries up, what will I use for drinking and washing?" After he finished speaking, the clear stream flowed abundantly, and soon the stream was full again. Both were contemporaries of Emperor Wu and Emperor Huizong. Zhi Daolin wrote an inscription for them: "Yulan transcended the world, summarizing the profound meanings. He retired to the mountains and lakes, and his kindness moved the tiger. Zhu Hu was calm and serene, with a profound and beautiful moral character. He softly sang in the empty valley, and the dried-up spring returned to flowing." From the Jewel Forest of the Dharma Garden, Chapter 63. [28]

    晋司空庐江何充，字次道，弱而信法，心业甚精。常于斋堂，置一空座，筵帐精华，络以珠宝；设之积年，庶降神异。后大会道俗，甚盛。坐次一僧，容服粗垢，神情低陋，出自众中，迳升其座，拱默而已，无所言说。一堂怪骇，谓其谬僻。充亦不平，嫌于颜色。及行中食，此僧饭于高座；饭毕，提钵出堂，顾谓充曰：“何侯徒劳精进！”因掷钵空中，陵空而去。充及道俗，驰遽观之：光仪伟丽，极目乃没。追共惋恨，稽忏累日。法苑珠林四十二 [29]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [29]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 弱而信法 - Weak in physical strength but firm in faith, indicating a strong commitment to religious or spiritual practices despite frail health.
    • 心業甚精 - His mind and industry were very refined, suggesting a high level of dedication and skill in his spiritual pursuits.
    • 置一空座 - Placed an empty seat, a ritual act often associated with inviting divine presence or spirits.
    • 筵帐精华，络以珠宝 - Adorned with fine fabrics and decorated with jewels, describing the elaborate and luxurious setup of the seat.
    • 设之积年 - Set it up for many years, indicating a long-term commitment to the ritual.
    • 庶降神异 - Hoping to attract divine or supernatural beings, expressing the expectation of a spiritual manifestation.
    • 大会道俗 - Held a grand assembly of both religious and secular people, indicating a significant gathering involving various social strata.
    • 坐次一僧 - One monk took a seat, describing the unexpected arrival of a monk.
    • 容服粗垢 - Wore rough and dirty clothing, indicating the monk's humble and unassuming appearance.
    • 神情低陋 - Had a low and unremarkable demeanor, suggesting the monk's lack of outward distinction.
    • 出自众中，迳升其座 - Came out of the crowd and directly ascended the seat, describing the monk's bold and unexpected action.
    • 拱默而已，无所言说 - Simply sat there in silence, saying nothing, indicating the monk's quiet and uncommunicative presence.
    • 一堂怪骇 - Everyone in the hall was startled, expressing the surprise and shock of the assembly.
    • 谓其谬僻 - Thought he was strange or eccentric, indicating the initial judgment of the monk's behavior.
    • 嫌于颜色 - Disliked his appearance, suggesting a negative reaction to the monk's looks.
    • 及行中食 - When it came time for lunch, indicating the transition to the meal.
    • 此僧饭于高座 - This monk ate at the high seat, describing the monk's continued presence at the elevated position.
    • 饭毕，提钵出堂 - After finishing his meal, he picked up his bowl and left the hall, indicating the monk's departure.
    • 顾谓充曰 - Turned to He Chong and said, describing the monk's direct address to He Chong.
    • 何侯徒劳精进 - Your Lordship has labored in vain, a critical statement suggesting that He Chong's efforts were futile.
    • 因掷钵空中，陵空而去 - Then he threw his bowl into the air and flew away, describing the monk's supernatural departure.
    • 充及道俗，驰遽观之 - He Chong and the assembly rushed to look, indicating their pursuit of the monk's departure.
    • 光仪伟丽，极目乃没 - They saw a magnificent and splendid figure, which disappeared into the distance, describing the monk's divine appearance.
    • 追共惋恨，稽忏累日 - They followed him with regret and performed penance for several days, indicating the lasting impact of the monk's message.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋司空庐江何充 - He Chong, the Sikan (a high-ranking official) of Lujiang during the Jin Dynasty, referring to a prominent historical figure.
    • 法苑珠林四十二 - Chapter 42 of *Fa Yuan Zhu Lin*, a historical and religious text that records this event.

    Proper Nouns

    • 晋司空庐江何充 - He Chong, a historical figure.
    • 法苑珠林四十二 - Chapter 42 of *Fa Yuan Zhu Lin*.

    He Chong, the Sikan of Lujiang during the Jin Dynasty, was weak in physical strength but firm in faith, and his mind and industry were very refined. He often placed an empty seat in his study hall, adorned with fine fabrics and decorated with jewels. He had set it up for many years, hoping to attract divine or supernatural beings. Later, he held a grand assembly of both religious and secular people, which was very impressive. Suddenly, one monk came out of the crowd and directly ascended the seat, simply sitting there in silence, saying nothing. Everyone in the hall was startled, thinking he was strange or eccentric. He Chong disliked his appearance.

    When it came time for lunch, this monk ate at the high seat. After finishing his meal, he picked up his bowl and left the hall, turning to He Chong and saying, "Your Lordship has labored in vain!" Then he threw his bowl into the air and flew away. He Chong and the assembly rushed to look, and they saw a magnificent and splendid figure, which disappeared into the distance. They followed him with regret and performed penance for several days. [29]

    晋尼竺道容，不知何许人。居于乌江寺。戒行精峻，屡有征感。晋明帝时，甚见敬事。以华藉席，验其所得；果不萎焉。时简文帝事清水道；所奉之师，即京师所谓王濮阳也。弟内其道舍，容亟开化；帝未之从。其后帝每入道屋，辄见神人为沙门形，盈满室内，帝疑容所为，因事为师。遂奉正法。晋氏显尚佛道，此尼力也。当时崇异，号为圣人。新林寺即帝为容所造也。孝武初，忽而绝迹，不知所在。乃葬其衣钵。故寺边有冢在焉。珠林四十二 [30]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [30]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 不知何許人 - A common phrase meaning 'unknown origin' or 'of unknown background.'
    • 戒行精峻 - Refers to strict and virtuous behavior, where '戒行' means 'virtuous conduct' and '精峻' means 'strict and refined.'
    • 屡有征感 - Means 'often has miraculous signs or omens.'
    • 以华藉席，验其所得 - A test to see if the person has supernatural abilities by placing a flower on a seat; if it doesn't wither, it indicates the person has such powers.
    • 果不萎焉 - Means 'indeed did not wither.'
    • 简文帝事清水道 - Refers to Sima Rui (Simpin), the emperor who followed the teachings of the Clear Water School (清水道).
    • 所奉之师，即京师所谓王濮阳也 - The teacher he followed was known as Wang Puyang in the capital.
    • 弟内其道舍 - '弟' here means 'his younger brother' or 'a follower,' and '内其道舍' means 'invited him into his hermitage.'
    • 容亟开化 - '亟' means 'urgently' or 'repeatedly,' and '开化' means 'to enlighten' or 'to teach.'
    • 帝未之从 - An archaic construction meaning 'the emperor did not follow his advice.'
    • 辄见神人为沙门形 - '辄' means 'always' or 'every time,' and '神人' means 'divine being' or 'spiritual figure.' '沙门形' means 'in the form of a monk.'
    • 帝疑容所为 - '疑' means 'suspected,' and '容所为' means 'what Nichiren did.'
    • 遂奉正法 - '遂' means 'then' or 'thereafter,' and '奉正法' means 'to follow the true Dharma.'
    • 晋氏显尚佛道 - '显尚' means 'to exalt' or 'to elevate,' and '佛道' means 'Buddhism.'
    • 此尼力也 - '此尼' means 'this nun,' and '力也' means 'is the power of.'
    • 当时崇异，号为圣人 - '崇异' means 'highly respected' or 'revered,' and '号为圣人' means 'was called a sage.'
    • 新林寺即帝为容所造也 - '新林寺' means 'Xinlin Temple,' '即' means 'is,' and '帝为容所造也' means 'built by the emperor for Nichiren.'
    • 孝武初，忽而绝迹 - '孝武' refers to Emperor Xiaowu of Jin, and '忽而绝迹' means 'suddenly disappeared.'
    • 不知所在 - Means 'no one knows where he is.'
    • 乃葬其衣钵 - '乃' means 'then' or 'therefore,' and '葬其衣钵' means 'buried his robes and begging bowl.'
    • 故寺边有冢在焉 - '故寺' means 'the old temple,' '边' means 'near,' '有冢' means 'there is a tomb,' and '在焉' means 'there.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋尼竺道容 - Nichiren, a Buddhist nun during the Jin dynasty.
    • 乌江寺 - A temple located near the Wu River.
    • 晋明帝 - Sima Shao, the third emperor of the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 简文帝 - Sima Rui, the first emperor of the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 清水道 - A sect of Buddhism, possibly referring to the Pure Land School.
    • 王濮阳 - A monk known as Wang Puyang in the capital.
    • 新林寺 - A temple built by Emperor Xiaowu of Jin for Nichiren.
    • 孝武帝 - Emperor Xiaowu of Jin, the ninth emperor of the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 佛道 - Buddhism, a major religion in China.

    Proper Nouns

    • 晋尼竺道容 - Nichiren, a Buddhist nun during the Jin dynasty.
    • 乌江寺 - A temple located near the Wu River.
    • 晋明帝 - Sima Shao, the third emperor of the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 简文帝 - Sima Rui, the first emperor of the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 清水道 - A sect of Buddhism, possibly referring to the Pure Land School.
    • 王濮阳 - A monk known as Wang Puyang in the capital.
    • 新林寺 - A temple built by Emperor Xiaowu of Jin for Nichiren.
    • 孝武帝 - Emperor Xiaowu of Jin, the ninth emperor of the Eastern Jin dynasty.

    Nichiren, a nun of the Jin dynasty, was of unknown origin. She resided at the Wujiang Temple. Known for her strict and virtuous conduct, she often exhibited miraculous signs. During the reign of Emperor Ming of Jin, she was greatly respected. To test her supernatural abilities, a flower was placed on a seat; it indeed did not wither. At the time, Emperor Jianwen followed the teachings of the Clear Water School, and his teacher was known as Wang Puyang in the capital. Nichiren was invited into his hermitage and repeatedly tried to enlighten him, but the emperor did not follow her advice. Later, whenever the emperor entered the hermitage, he would always see divine beings appearing in the form of monks, filling the room. He suspected that Nichiren was responsible and thus took her as his teacher. Consequently, he followed the true Dharma. The Jin dynasty elevated Buddhism, largely due to Nichiren's efforts. She was highly revered and called a sage. Xinlin Temple was built by Emperor Xiaowu for Nichiren. At the beginning of Emperor Xiaowu's reign, Nichiren suddenly disappeared, and no one knew where she had gone. Her robes and begging bowl were buried, and a tomb can be found near the temple. [30]

    晋阙公则，赵人也。恬放萧然，唯勤法事。法事感通录引作法华。晋武之世，死于洛阳。道俗同志，为设会于白马寺中。其夕转经。宵分，闻空中有唱赞声。仰见一人，形器壮伟，仪服整丽乃言曰：“我是阙公则，今生西方安乐世界，与诸菩萨共来听经。”合堂惊跃，皆得睹见。时复有汲郡卫士度，亦苦行居士也。师于公则。其母又甚信尚，诵经长斋，家常饭僧。时日将中，母出斋堂，与诸尼僧，逍遥眺望。忽见空中有一物下，正落母前，乃则钵也；有饭盈焉，馨气充?。○堂肃然，一时礼敬。母自分行斋人食之，皆七日不饥。此钵犹云尚存此土。度善有文辞，作八关忏文。晋末斋者尚用之，晋永昌中死，亦见灵异。有浩像者作圣贤传，具载其事云。度亦生西方。吴兴王该日。烛曰：“阙夐登霄，卫度继轨。咸恬泊以无生，俱蜕骸以不死”者也。珠林四十二三宝感通录四 [31]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [31]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 晋阙公则，赵人也 - Standard biographical formula meaning 'Jin Quègōngzé was from Zhao.'
    • 恬放萧然，唯勤法事 - Describes a state of carefree and detached disposition, only diligent in religious practices.
    • 法事感通录引作法华 - Indicates that the text was cited in the *Fashì Gǎntōng Lù* as the *Fǎhuá* (Lotus Sutra).
    • 其夕转经，宵分，闻空中有唱赞声 - That night, they recited scriptures; at midnight, they heard a singing and praising sound in the air.
    • 仰见一人，形器壮伟，仪服整丽乃言曰 - Describes seeing a person with a majestic appearance and well-dressed attire, then said.
    • 我是阙公则，今生西方安乐世界，与诸菩萨共来听经 - I am Quègōngzé, and I have been reborn in the Western Blissful World, coming with the Bodhisattvas to listen to the sutras.
    • 合堂惊跃，皆得睹见 - Everyone in the hall was shocked and jumped up, all able to see him.
    • 时复有汲郡卫士度，亦苦行居士也 - There was also Wei Shìdù from Jǐ County, who was also a practicing layperson.
    • 师于公则 - He learned from Quègōngzé.
    • 其母又甚信尚，诵经长斋，家常饭僧 - His mother was very devout, recited scriptures, observed long fasts, and regularly fed monks.
    • 时日将中，母出斋堂，与诸尼僧，逍遥眺望 - One day, she went out of the斋堂, accompanied by nuns and monks, and strolled around.
    • 忽见空中有一物下，正落母前，乃则钵也；有饭盈焉，馨气充? - Suddenly, she saw something fall from the air, landing right in front of her, which was Quègōngzé's bowl; it was full of rice, and the fragrance filled the air.
    • 珠林四十二三宝感通录四 - Refers to the *Zhūlín* (Jewel Forest) and *Sānbǎo Gǎntōng Lù* (Records of the Marvelous Connections of the Three Treasures), indicating the source of the story.
    • 度亦生西方 - Wei Shìdù also was reborn in the West.
    • 吴兴王该日。烛曰 - Wúxīng Wang Gāi said on that day.
    • 阙夐登霄，卫度继轨 - Quègōngzé ascended to heaven, and Wei Shìdù followed in his footsteps.
    • 咸恬泊以无生，俱蜕骸以不死 - Both were tranquil and detached, transcending life and death.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋阙公则 - A historical figure from the Jin dynasty known for his religious devotion.
    • 赵人也 - Indicates that Quègōngzé was from the state of Zhao.
    • 晋武之世 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Wu of Jin (265–290 AD).
    • 洛阳 - The capital of the Jin dynasty, now modern-day Luoyang in Henan Province.
    • 白马寺 - The White Horse Temple, one of the earliest Buddhist temples in China, located in Luoyang.
    • 法华 - The *Fǎhuá* (Lotus Sutra), a major Mahayana Buddhist text.
    • 西方安乐世界 - The Western Blissful World, a Buddhist paradise.
    • 汲郡 - A county in Henan Province.
    • 卫士度 - A historical figure, likely a lay practitioner of Buddhism.
    • 吴兴 - A region in Zhejiang Province.
    • 王该 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official.
    • 珠林 - The *Zhūlín* (Jewel Forest), a collection of Buddhist stories and legends.
    • 三宝感通录 - The *Sānbǎo Gǎntōng Lù* (Records of the Marvelous Connections of the Three Treasures), a collection of Buddhist miracles and supernatural events.

    Proper Nouns

    • 晋阙公则 - Quègōngzé, a historical figure from the Jin dynasty.
    • 赵 - Zhao, a state during the Warring States period.
    • 晋武之世 - The reign of Emperor Wu of Jin.
    • 洛阳 - Luoyang, the capital of the Jin dynasty.
    • 白马寺 - The White Horse Temple, a famous Buddhist temple in Luoyang.
    • 法华 - The *Fǎhuá* (Lotus Sutra), a major Mahayana Buddhist text.
    • 西方安乐世界 - The Western Blissful World, a Buddhist paradise.
    • 汲郡 - Jǐ County, a county in Henan Province.
    • 卫士度 - Wei Shìdù, a historical figure.
    • 吴兴 - Wúxīng, a region in Zhejiang Province.
    • 王该 - Wang Gāi, a historical figure.
    • 珠林 - The *Zhūlín* (Jewel Forest), a collection of Buddhist stories and legends.
    • 三宝感通录 - The *Sānbǎo Gǎntōng Lù* (Records of the Marvelous Connections of the Three Treasures), a collection of Buddhist miracles and supernatural events.

    Quègōngzé of Jin, from Zhao, was a man of carefree and detached disposition, devoted only to religious practices. The Fashì Gǎntōng Lù (Records of the Marvelous Connections in Religious Practices) cites this as the Fǎhuá (Lotus Sutra). During the reign of Emperor Wu of Jin, he died in Luoyang. Both monks and laypeople who shared his faith held a gathering at the White Horse Temple. That night, they recited scriptures. At midnight, they heard a singing and praising sound in the air. Looking up, they saw a person with a majestic appearance and well-dressed attire, who then said, "I am Quègōngzé, and I have been reborn in the Western Blissful World, coming with the Bodhisattvas to listen to the sutras." Everyone in the hall was shocked and jumped up, all able to see him. There was also Wei Shìdù from Jǐ County, who was also a practicing layperson and learned from Quègōngzé. His mother was very devout, recited scriptures, observed long fasts, and regularly fed monks. One day, she went out of the斋堂, accompanied by nuns and monks, and strolled around. Suddenly, she saw something fall from the air, landing right in front of her, which was Quègōngzé's bowl; it was full of rice, and the fragrance filled the air. The hall was solemn, and everyone paid respects. She distributed the food among the monks, and all of them did not feel hungry for seven days. This bowl is said to still exist in this world. Wei Shìdù was skilled in writing and composed the Bāguān Chàn (Eight Gates Repentance) text. In the late Jin dynasty, people still used it. He died in Yongchang during the Jin dynasty and was also seen with supernatural phenomena. The Shèngxiánzhuàn (Biographies of the Saints) by Hào Xiàng records these events. Wei Shìdù also was reborn in the West. Wang Gāi of Wúxīng said on that day, "Quègōngzé ascended to heaven, and Wei Shìdù followed in his footsteps. Both were tranquil and detached, transcending life and death." Zhūlín (Jewel Forest) Vol. 42, Sānbǎo Gǎntōng Lù (Records of the Marvelous Connections of the Three Treasures) Vol. 4. [31]

    晋南阳滕普，累世敬信。妻吴郡全氏，尤能精苦。每设斋会，不逆招请；随有来者，因留供之。后会僧数阙少，使人衢路要寻。见一沙门，荫柳而坐，因请与归。净人行食，翻饭于地；倾箪都尽，罔然无计。此沙门云：“贫道钵中有饭，足供一众。”使普分行，既而道俗内外，皆得充饱。清净既毕，掷钵空中，翻然上升。极目乃灭。普即刻木作其形像，朝夕拜礼。普家将有凶祸，则此像必先倒踣云。普子含，以苏峻之功封东兴者也。珠林四十二 [32]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [32]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 累世敬信 - A phrase meaning 'many generations of reverence and faith,' indicating a long-standing tradition of piety.
    • 設齋會 - Refers to the act of setting up a vegetarian feast or gathering, often for religious or charitable purposes.
    • 不逆招請 - Means 'not to actively invite' or 'not to solicit,' suggesting a passive approach to hosting such events.
    • 隨有來者，因留供之 - A phrase meaning 'whenever someone comes, they are invited to stay and be served,' indicating hospitality.
    • 淨人行食，翻飯於地 - A phrase describing a situation where a servant accidentally spills food, using the verb '翻' (to overturn) to describe the mishap.
    • 傾簞都盡 - Means 'to completely empty the basket,' indicating a significant amount of food was spilled.
    • 罔然無計 - A phrase meaning 'helpless and without a plan,' describing the state of being at a loss.
    • 貧道 - A humble self-reference used by monks, meaning 'this humble monk.'
    • 足供一眾 - Means 'enough to feed everyone,' indicating the monk's ability to provide for the group.
    • 淨人分行 - Refers to the act of distributing food, with '分行' meaning 'to distribute.'
    • 極目乃滅 - A phrase meaning 'disappears from sight,' indicating the monk's ascent into the sky.
    • 刻木作其形像 - Means 'carved a wooden statue,' indicating the creation of a physical representation.
    • 倒踣 - A phrase meaning 'to fall over,' describing the statue's behavior.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晉 - Refers to the Jin Dynasty, a period in Chinese history from 266 to 420 CE.
    • 南陽滕普 - The name of the protagonist, a man from Nanyang who is known for his piety.
    • 吳郡全氏 - The name of the protagonist's wife, a woman from Wu County who is known for her devotion.
    • 沙門 - Refers to Buddhist monks, indicating the religious context of the story.
    • 淨人 - Refers to servants or attendants in a monastery, often responsible for serving food.
    • 東興 - A title granted to individuals for their military achievements, indicating a high level of recognition and reward.
    • 珠林四十二 - Refers to the source of the text, likely a collection of stories or records.

    Proper Nouns

    • 晉 - The Jin Dynasty.
    • 南陽滕普 - The protagonist, a man from Nanyang.
    • 吳郡全氏 - The protagonist's wife, a woman from Wu County.
    • 沙门 - Buddhist monks.
    • 淨人 - Servants or attendants in a monastery.
    • 東興 - A title granted for military achievements.
    • 珠林四十二 - The source of the story, likely a collection of records or stories.

    During the Jin Dynasty, there was a man named Teng Pu from Nanyang who had many generations of reverence and faith. His wife, Quan from Wu County, was especially devoted and could endure great hardships. They often set up vegetarian feasts without actively inviting guests; whenever someone came, they would invite them to stay and be served. One day, when they were hosting a gathering for monks and there were not enough of them, they sent someone to the road to look for more. They saw a monk sitting under a willow tree, so they invited him to return with them. When the servant was serving food, he accidentally spilled it on the ground, completely emptying the basket, leaving them helpless and without a plan. The monk said, "This humble monk has food in his bowl that is enough to feed everyone." They had the monk distribute the food, and afterward, both the monks and laypeople inside and outside the gathering were fully satisfied. After the meal, the monk threw his bowl into the air and ascended into the sky, disappearing from sight. Teng Pu then carved a wooden statue of the monk and worshipped it daily. Whenever Teng Pu's family was about to face misfortune, the statue would fall over first. Teng Pu's son, Han, was granted the title of Dongxing for his military achievements against Su Jun. This story is recorded in Zhu Lin Volume 42. [32]

    沙门竺法进者，开度浮图主也。聪达多知，能解殊俗之言。京洛将乱，欲处山泽。众人请留，进皆不听。大会烧香，与众告别。临当布香，忽有一僧来，处上座：衣服尘垢，面目黄肿。法进怪贱，牵就下次；辄复来上。牵之至三，乃不复见。众坐既定，方就下食，忽暴风扬沙，柈桉倾倒。法进忏悔自责；乃止不入山。时论以为：世将大乱，法进不宜入山。又道俗至意，苦相留慕，故见此神异，止其行意也。珠林四十二 [33]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [33]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 沙门 - Refers to Buddhist monks, a term used in Chinese Buddhism.
    • 竺法进 - A proper name, likely the name of a specific monk.
    • 开度浮图主 - A title or position within a Buddhist monastery, possibly referring to a leader or head of a temple.
    • 聪达多知 - Describes someone who is intelligent, knowledgeable, and well-informed.
    • 能解殊俗之言 - Means 'able to understand foreign languages' or 'capable of interpreting different dialects.'
    • 京洛将乱 - Indicates that the capital city (likely Luoyang) is about to face turmoil or chaos.
    • 欲处山泽 - Means 'wants to live in the mountains and wilderness,' suggesting a desire for seclusion.
    • 众人请留 - Means 'the people ask him to stay.'
    • 进皆不听 - Means 'Zhu Fa Jin refuses to listen.'
    • 大会烧香 - Refers to a large gathering where incense is burned, often a ritual or ceremony.
    • 与众告别 - Means 'to bid farewell to the crowd.'
    • 临当布香 - Means 'when he is about to distribute incense.'
    • 忽有一僧来 - Means 'suddenly a monk appears.'
    • 处上座 - Means 'sits in the upper seat,' indicating a position of respect.
    • 衣服尘垢 - Means 'clothes are dusty and dirty.'
    • 面目黄肿 - Describes a face that is yellow and swollen, possibly indicating illness or spiritual transformation.
    • 法进怪贱 - Means 'Zhu Fa Jin finds him strange and lowly.'
    • 牵就下次 - Means 'pulls him to a lower seat.'
    • 辄复来上 - Means 'he repeatedly returns to the upper seat.'
    • 牵之至三 - Means 'he pulls him three times.'
    • 乃不复见 - Means 'he then disappears.'
    • 众坐既定 - Means 'the crowd has settled down.'
    • 方就下食 - Means 'only then does he eat at the lower seat.'
    • 忽暴风扬沙 - Means 'suddenly a strong wind blows sand.'
    • 柈桉倾倒 - Means 'the trays and tables tip over.'
    • 法进忏悔自责 - Means 'Zhu Fa Jin repents and blames himself.'
    • 乃止不入山 - Means 'he then stops and does not enter the mountains.'
    • 时论以为 - Means 'people at the time believe.'
    • 世将大乱 - Means 'the world is about to be in great turmoil.'
    • 法进不宜入山 - Means 'Zhu Fa Jin should not enter the mountains.'
    • 道俗至意 - Refers to both Buddhist monks and laypeople who are deeply concerned.
    • 苦相留慕 - Means 'they earnestly try to persuade and admire him.'
    • 故见此神异 - Means 'therefore, this supernatural event occurs.'
    • 止其行意也 - Means 'to stop his intention to leave.'
    • 珠林四十二 - Refers to a specific chapter or section in a Buddhist text, likely the *Zhu Lin* (Treasury of Jewels).

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 沙门 - Refers to Buddhist monks, a term used in Chinese Buddhism.
    • 竺法进 - A proper name, likely the name of a specific monk.
    • 开度浮图主 - A title or position within a Buddhist monastery, possibly referring to a leader or head of a temple.
    • 京洛 - Refers to the capital city, likely Luoyang, which was an important city during the Eastern Han and Wei-Jin periods.
    • 山泽 - Refers to mountains and wilderness, symbolizing seclusion and retreat.
    • 殊俗之言 - Refers to foreign languages or different dialects, indicating Zhu Fa Jin's ability to communicate with diverse groups.
    • 京洛将乱 - Indicates that the capital city is about to face turmoil or chaos, likely due to political or social unrest.
    • 大会烧香 - Refers to a large gathering where incense is burned, often a ritual or ceremony.
    • 与众告别 - Refers to a formal farewell, often associated with religious or significant events.
    • 上座 - Refers to a seat of honor, often given to respected individuals.
    • 尘垢 - Refers to dust and dirt, often used metaphorically to describe someone's appearance or state.
    • 黄肿 - Describes a yellow and swollen face, possibly indicating illness or spiritual transformation.
    • 道俗 - Refers to both Buddhist monks (道) and laypeople (俗).
    • 神异 - Refers to supernatural or miraculous events.
    • 珠林四十二 - Refers to a specific chapter or section in a Buddhist text, likely the *Zhu Lin* (Treasury of Jewels), which is a collection of Buddhist stories and teachings.

    Proper Nouns

    • 竺法进 - A proper name, likely the name of a specific monk.
    • 京洛 - Refers to the capital city, likely Luoyang.
    • 珠林四十二 - Refers to a specific chapter or section in a Buddhist text, likely the *Zhu Lin* (Treasury of Jewels).

    The Monk Zhu Fa Jin, the head of the Kai Du Floating Pagoda, was intelligent, knowledgeable, and capable of understanding foreign languages. As the capital city was about to face turmoil, he wished to live in the mountains and wilderness. The people asked him to stay, but he refused to listen.
