    • 临湘令 - The position of magistrate in Linxiang.
    • 友人司马逊 - Sima Xun, his friend.
    • 经教 - Religious or philosophical teachings.
    • 圣人 - Sage or wise person.
    • 幽途 - The afterlife or underworld.

    After many years following his death, Yuan Bing, a man from Chen County, served as the magistrate of Linxiang at the end of the Tai Shi era. His friend Sima Xun, in a dream-like state just before dawn, saw Yuan Bing appear to him. Yuan Bing recounted their long separation and inquired about his well-being. He then said, "Throughout our lives, we often discussed and held the view that life is a race, while death is rest. Now I know for the first time that the extensive records and quotations containing the word 'know' are certainly not correct. I was always troubled by people in the world who are busy chasing after money and gold, exchanging gifts with each other. In the afterlife, this situation is also the same."

    Sima Xun asked, "Are the rewards and punishments for sins and virtues truly determined?" Yuan Bing replied, "As I saw it before, it does not fully agree with what is said in the scriptures. This is probably the sage's way of guiding and restraining. The general categories of good and evil are not very different. However, killing living beings is the most serious, and it must be strictly forbidden and not violated." [142]

    ”逊曰：卿此征相示，良不可言，当以语白尚书也。”炳曰：“甚善，亦请卿敬情尚书。”时司空简穆王公广记引作时司空王僧虔为吏部尚书，炳逊并其游宾，故及之。往返可数百语，辞去，逊曰：“阔别之久，恒思叙集，相值甚难，何不小住？”炳曰：“止暂来耳！不可得久留。且此辈语亦不容得委悉。”于是而去。初炳来闇夜，逊亦了不觉所以而明得睹见炳，既去，逊下床送之，始蹑屐而还闇，见炳脚间有光可尺许，示得照其两足，余地犹皆闇云。珠林二十一广记三百二十六 [143]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [143]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 卿此征相示 - Formal expression meaning 'you show this sign to me.' '征' can mean a sign or omen, and '相示' means to show or indicate.
    • 良不可言 - Phrase meaning 'it is indeed hard to put into words' or 'it is truly difficult to express.'
    • 当以语白尚书 - Means 'you should inform the Shangshu (Minister of Works).' '语白' means to inform or report.
    • 甚善 - Polite expression meaning 'very good' or 'excellent.'
    • 亦请卿敬情尚书 - Means 'also please inform the Shangshu with respect.' '敬情' means to show respect or courtesy.
    • 止暂来耳 - Phrase meaning 'I just came briefly.'
    • 不可得久留 - Means 'cannot stay for long.'
    • 此辈语亦不容得委悉 - Means 'the words of such people cannot be fully understood' or 'the talk of these people cannot be fully explained.'
    • 闇夜 - Refers to darkness or nighttime.
    • 了不觉所以而明得睹见炳 - Phrase meaning 'without knowing why, I was able to see clearly.'
    • 始蹑屐而还闇 - Means 'I began to walk in sandals back into the darkness.'
    • 脚间有光可尺许 - Means 'there was light about a foot long between his feet.'
    • 示得照其两足 - Means 'it illuminated only his two feet.'
    • 余地犹皆闇云 - Means 'the rest of the place was still dark.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 司空 - A high-ranking official in ancient China, often responsible for civil engineering and public works.
    • 简穆王公 - A historical figure, likely a noble or official of high rank.
    • 广记 - A reference to a historical or literary work, possibly a collection of anecdotes or records.
    • 吏部尚书 - The head of the Ministry of Personnel, responsible for appointments and administration.
    • 游宾 - Refers to guests or visitors who are treated as honored guests.
    • 珠林 - A reference to a historical or literary work, possibly a collection of stories or records.

    Proper Nouns

    • 卿 - A respectful term for 'you.'
    • 尚书 - The Shangshu (Minister of Works).
    • 简穆王公 - A historical figure, likely a noble or official of high rank.
    • 吏部尚书 - The head of the Ministry of Personnel.
    • 游宾 - Guests or visitors treated as honored guests.
    • 珠林 - A historical or literary work, possibly a collection of stories or records.
    • 广记 - Another historical or literary work, possibly a collection of anecdotes or records.

    "Xun said, 'Your sign that you have shown me is truly difficult to put into words; you should inform the Shangshu (Minister of Works).' Bing replied, 'Very good, also please inform the Shangshu with respect.' At that time, the high-ranking official Jianmu Wang Gong (as recorded in Guangji), or Wang Sengqian as the Guangji states, was the Shangshu of the Ministry of Personnel, and Bing and Xun were among his honored guests, hence this conversation took place. They exchanged several hundred words before parting. Xun said, 'It has been a long time since we last met, and I have always thought of gathering and talking; it is very difficult to meet, so why don't you stay a little longer?' Bing said, 'I just came briefly! I cannot stay for long. Moreover, the words of such people cannot be fully understood.' So he left. Initially, when Bing arrived in the darkness, Xun did not know why but was able to see clearly. After Bing left, Xun got out of bed to see him off, and as he began to walk in sandals back into the darkness, he saw light about a foot long between Bing's feet, illuminating only his two feet, while the rest of the place was still dark. Zhulin 21, Guangji 326." [143]

    宋沙门道志者，北多宝僧也，尝为众僧，为众二字依广记引补令知殿塔，自窃帐盖等宝饰，所取甚众。后遂偷像眉间珠相，既而开穿垣壁，若外盗者，故僧众不能觉也。积旬余而得病，便见异人以戈矛刺之，时来时去，来辄惊噭，应声流血。初犹日中一两如此，其后疾甚，刺者稍数，伤痍遍体，呻呼不能绝声。同寺僧众，颇疑其有罪，欲为忏谢，始问犹讳而不言，将尽二三日，乃具自陈列，泣涕请救，曰：“吾愚悖不通，谓无幽途，失意作罪，招此殃酷，生受楚拷，死萦刀镬，已糜之身，唯垂哀恕。 [144]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [144]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 宋沙门道志者 - A monk named Daozhi from the Song dynasty.
    • 北多宝僧也 - He was a monk from the Northern Dabao temple.
    • 为众僧 - For the sake of the monks, indicating a purpose or role.
    • 令知殿塔 - To manage the temple and pagoda.
    • 自窃帐盖等宝饰 - He secretly stole the canopy and other precious decorations.
    • 所取甚众 - He took a great deal.
    • 偷像眉间珠相 - He stole the pearl in the Buddha statue's eyebrow.
    • 开穿垣壁 - He dug through the walls.
    • 若外盗者 - As if it were an external thief.
    • 故僧众不能觉也 - So the monks did not notice.
    • 积旬余而得病 - After more than ten days, he fell ill.
    • 便见异人以戈矛刺之 - He then saw a strange person stabbing him with spears.
    • 时来时去 - Coming and going at times.
    • 来辄惊噭 - Coming and causing him to scream.
    • 应声流血 - Blood flowing in response to the screams.
    • 初犹日中一两如此 - At first, it happened once or twice a day.
    • 其后疾甚 - Later, his illness worsened.
    • 刺者稍数 - The number of stabbings increased.
    • 伤痍遍体 - Wounds all over his body.
    • 呻呼不能绝声 - He moaned and could not stop.
    • 同寺僧众 - The monks of the same temple.
    • 颇疑其有罪 - They suspected he had committed a crime.
    • 欲为忏谢 - They wanted to perform a repentance ceremony.
    • 始问犹讳而不言 - At first, he was still reluctant to speak.
    • 将尽二三日 - As he was nearing death, two or three days later.
    • 乃具自陈列 - He then fully confessed.
    • 泣涕请救 - He wept and begged for mercy.
    • 吾愚悖不通 - I am foolish and ignorant.
    • 谓无幽途 - Believing there was no afterlife.
    • 失意作罪 - Acting out of anger and committing a sin.
    • 招此殃酷 - Bringing this suffering upon myself.
    • 生受楚拷 - Suffering torture in life.
    • 死萦刀镬 - Being tormented in death.
    • 已糜之身 - My ruined body.
    • 唯垂哀恕 - Only beg for pity and forgiveness.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 宋 - Refers to the Song dynasty, a period in Chinese history.
    • 沙门 - Refers to a Buddhist monk.
    • 北多宝 - Refers to the Northern Dabao temple, a specific temple in the Song dynasty.
    • 殿塔 - Refers to the temple and pagoda, important structures in Buddhist monasteries.
    • 帐盖等宝饰 - Refers to the canopy and other precious decorations, valuable items in the temple.
    • 像眉间珠相 - Refers to the pearl in the Buddha statue's eyebrow, a significant and valuable part of the statue.
    • 垣壁 - Refers to the walls of the temple, physical barriers that were breached.
    • 异人 - Refers to a strange or supernatural person, often associated with ghosts or spirits in Chinese literature.
    • 戈矛 - Refers to spears, weapons used in the dream or vision.
    • 僧众 - Refers to the monks of the same temple, the community of religious practitioners.
    • 忏谢 - Refers to a repentance ceremony, a ritual to seek forgiveness for sins.
    • 幽途 - Refers to the afterlife, the spiritual realm beyond death.
    • 楚拷 - Refers to torture, a form of punishment or suffering.
    • 刀镬 - Refers to torment, a metaphorical reference to the afterlife punishment.

    Proper Nouns

    • 宋沙门道志者 - A monk named Daozhi from the Song dynasty.
    • 北多宝僧也 - He was a monk from the Northern Dabao temple.
    • 北多宝 - The Northern Dabao temple.
    • 像眉间珠相 - The pearl in the Buddha statue's eyebrow.

    A monk named Daozhi from the Song dynasty, who was from the Northern Dabao temple, once served as a caretaker for the temple and pagoda. He secretly stole the canopy and other precious decorations, taking a great deal of them. Later, he stole the pearl from the Buddha statue's eyebrow. He then dug through the walls, as if it were an external thief, so the monks did not notice. After more than ten days, he fell ill and saw a strange person stabbing him with spears. The person came and went at times, and whenever they came, he would scream and blood would flow in response. At first, this happened once or twice a day, but later his illness worsened, and the number of stabbings increased, causing wounds all over his body. He moaned and could not stop. The monks of the same temple suspected he had committed a crime and wanted to perform a repentance ceremony. At first, he was still reluctant to speak, but as he was nearing death, two or three days later, he fully confessed and wept, begging for mercy. He said, "I am foolish and ignorant, believing there was no afterlife. Acting out of anger, I committed a sin, bringing this suffering upon myself. I suffer torture in life and am tormented in death. My ruined body, I only beg for pity and forgiveness." [144]

    今无复余物，唯衣被毡履，或足充一会，并烦请愿具为，忏悔。昔偷像相珠有二枚，一枚已属妪人，不可复得，一以质钱，在陈照家，今可赎取。”道志既死，诸僧合集赎得相珠，并设斋忏，初，工人复相珠时，展转迥趣，终不安合，众僧复为礼拜烧香，乃得着焉。年余而同学等于昏夜闲闻空中有语，详听即道志声也。自说云：自死以来，备萦痛毒，方累年劫，未有出期；赖蒙众僧，哀怜救护，赎像相珠，故于苦酷之中，时有闲息。感恩罔已，故暂来称谢，言此而已。闻其语时，腥腐臭气，苦痛难过，言终久久，臭乃稍歇。此事在泰始末年，其寺好事者，已具条记。珠林七十九广记一百十六 [145]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [145]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 今无复余物 - Literally means 'now there is nothing left,' indicating a state of poverty or scarcity.
    • 唯衣被毡履 - Refers to basic necessities: clothes, bedding, and shoes.
    • 或足充一会 - Means 'perhaps enough to sustain a single gathering' or 'just enough for one occasion.'
    • 并烦请愿具为，忏悔 - A formal request for others to perform a ritual of confession or repentance.
    • 昔偷像相珠有二枚 - Refers to a pair of stolen jewels (likely from a statue or image).
    • 一枚已属妪人 - Means 'one has already belonged to an old woman,' indicating the loss of one of the jewels.
    • 一以质钱，在陈照家 - Means 'the other was pawned for money and is now in the possession of Chen Zao.'
    • 今可赎取 - Means 'now it can be redeemed' or 'can be bought back.'
    • 道志既死 - Indicates the death of Dao Zhi, a monk or person mentioned in the text.
    • 诸僧合集赎得相珠 - Means 'the monks collectively gathered to redeem the jewels.'
    • 并设斋忏 - Refers to a ritual meal (斋) and confession (忏) performed by the monks.
    • 工人复相珠时，展转迥趣，终不安合 - Describes a physical phenomenon where the jewels, when reassembled, fail to fit properly.
    • 众僧复为礼拜烧香 - Means 'the monks then performed prayers and burned incense.'
    • 乃得着焉 - Means 'and finally, they were able to fit' or 'they were successfully reassembled.'
    • 年余而同学等于昏夜闲闻空中有语 - Describes a supernatural event where fellow students hear voices in the air during the night.
    • 详听即道志声也 - Means 'upon careful listening, it was the voice of Dao Zhi.'
    • 自说云：自死以来，备萦痛毒，方累年劫，未有出期 - Means 'he said: Since my death, I have been enduring pain and suffering, enduring countless years, with no end in sight.'
    • 赖蒙众僧，哀怜救护，赎像相珠，故于苦酷之中，时有闲息 - Means 'Thanks to the compassion and rescue of the monks, who redeemed the jewels, I occasionally find brief respite from my suffering.'
    • 感恩罔已，故暂来称谢，言此而已 - Means 'I am endlessly grateful, so I have come temporarily to express my thanks, saying only this.'
    • 闻其语时，腥腐臭气，苦痛难过 - Describes the unpleasant smell and suffering associated with the voice.
    • 言终久久，臭乃稍歇 - Means 'After he finished speaking, the odor gradually faded.'
    • 此事在泰始末年 - Indicates that the event took place in the late years of the Tai Shi era (a period in Chinese history).
    • 其寺好事者，已具条记 - Means 'those who are well-intentioned in the temple have already recorded it in detail.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 泰始末年 - Refers to the late years of the Tai Shi era, a period during the Eastern Jin Dynasty (317–420 CE).
    • 珠林七十九 - Refers to the *Jade Forest* (珠林), a Buddhist text, specifically Chapter 79.
    • 广记一百十六 - Refers to the *Comprehensive Records* (广记), a historical text, specifically Chapter 116.

    Proper Nouns

    • 道志 - The name of a monk or person mentioned in the text.
    • 陈照 - The name of a person who pawned one of the jewels.
    • 珠林 - The title of a Buddhist text.
    • 广记 - The title of a historical text.

    Now, using the research and context we gathered above, produce a final, polished translation of the source text.

    Your goal is to deliver a final English translation that is both faithful to the original Classical Chinese and reads naturally in English. Apply the insights from the pre-drafting research directly — handle idiomatic expressions, cultural allusions, syntactic ambiguities, and proper nouns as you planned.

    Here are 5 examples showing the expected translation quality and style:

    Example 1

    Source: 爰及農商工賈，廝役奴隸，釣魚屠肉，飯牛牧羊，皆有先達，可為師表，博學求之，無不利於事也。

    Translation: As for farmers, merchants, artisans, and traders, as well as servants, laborers, and slaves, those engaged in fishing and butchering, feeding oxen and herding sheep—all have predecessors who can serve as role models. By seeking knowledge broadly from them, nothing is unhelpful in one's endeavors.

    Example 2

    Source: 兩頭四臂女

    靈帝時，洛陽女子生時兩頭四臂。

    Translation: A Woman with Two Heads and Four Arms

    During the reign of Emperor Ling, a woman from Luoyang was born with two heads and four arms.

    Example 3

    Source: 凡為文章，猶乘騏驥，雖有逸氣，當以銜勒制之，勿使流亂軌躅，放意填坑岸也。

    Translation: In all writing, it is like riding a swift steed — though it may possess a wild and unrestrained spirit, one must control it with bit and bridle, and must not let it run wild, straying from the proper path or indulging in aimless wanderings.

    Example 4

    Source: 自古明王聖帝，猶須勤學，況凡庶乎！此事遍於經史，吾亦不能鄭重，聊舉近世切要，以啟寤汝耳。

    Translation: From ancient times, wise kings and sage emperors still required diligent study; how much more so, then, for common people! This matter is found throughout the classics and histories. I cannot elaborate further, but I will briefly mention a few essential points of recent times in order to enlighten you.

    Example 5

    Source: 夫文章者，原出五經：詔命策檄，生於書者也；序述論議，生於易者也；歌詠賦頌，生於詩者也；祭祀哀誄，生於禮者也；書奏箴銘，生於春秋者也。朝廷憲章，軍旅誓誥，敷顯仁義，發明功德，牧民建國，不可暫無。至於陶冶性靈，從容諷諫，入其滋味，亦樂事也。行有餘力，則可習之。

    Translation: As for writings, their origin lies in the Five Classics: edicts, memorials, and military proclamations arise from the Shujing (Book of Documents); expository and argumentative writings arise from the Yijing (Book of Changes); songs, odes, and praises arise from the Shijing (Book of Poetry); ritual sacrifices, mourning, and elegies arise from the Liji (Book of Rites); memorials, admonitions, and inscriptions arise from the Chunqiu (Spring and Autumn Annals). Court regulations, military oaths and decrees, the proclamation of benevolence and righteousness, the elucidation of virtue and merit, governing the people and establishing the state—all are indispensable. As for cultivating the mind and spirit, offering gentle admonition in a relaxed manner, and savoring the flavor of such writings, these too are pleasant pursuits. When one has spare energy, then one may study them.

    Source text:
    今无复余物，唯衣被毡履，或足充一会，并烦请愿具为，忏悔。昔偷像相珠有二枚，一枚已属妪人，不可复得，一以质钱，在陈照家，今可赎取。”道志既死，诸僧合集赎得相珠，并设斋忏，初，工人复相珠时，展转迥趣，终不安合，众僧复为礼拜烧香，乃得着焉。年余而同学等于昏夜闲闻空中有语，详听即道志声也。自说云：自死以来，备萦痛毒，方累年劫，未有出期；赖蒙众僧，哀怜救护，赎像相珠，故于苦酷之中，时有闲息。感恩罔已，故暂来称谢，言此而已。闻其语时，腥腐臭气，苦痛难过，言终久久，臭乃稍歇。此事在泰始末年，其寺好事者，已具条记。珠林七十九广记一百十六

    Provide only the final translation with no additional commentary. [145]

    宋陈秀远者，颍川人也，尝为湘州西曹，客居临湘县。少信奉三宝，年过耳顺，笃业不衰。宋元徽二年，七月中，于昏夕闲，闲卧未寝，叹念万品死生，流转无定，自惟已身，将从何来，一心祈念，冀通感梦。时夕结阴，室无灯烛，有顷，见枕边如萤火者，冏然明照，流飞而去。俄而一室尽明，爰至空中，有如朝昼。秀远遽起坐，合掌端念。顷，见中宁四五丈上，有一桥阁焉，又阑槛朱彩，立于空中，秀远了不觉升动之时，而已自见平坐桥侧。见桥上士女，往返填衢，衣服妆束，不异世人。末有一妪，年可三十许，上着青袄，下服白布裳，行至秀远左边而立；有顷，复有一妇人，通体衣白布，为偏环髻，手持华香，当前而立。语秀远曰：“汝欲睹前身，即我是也，以此华供养佛故，故得转身作汝。”回指白妪曰：“此即复是我先身也。”言毕而去，去后桥亦渐隐。秀远忽然不觉还下之时，光亦寻灭也。珠林三十二广记一百十四 [146]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [146]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 宋陈秀远者，颍川人也 - Introduces the subject, Cen Xiu Yuan, from Yen-chuan.
    • 少信奉三宝 - Refers to the reverence for the Three Jewels of Buddhism (Buddha, Dharma, Sangha) in one's youth.
    • 年过耳顺，笃业不衰 - Means to remain steadfast in one's occupation or pursuit after reaching the age of 60.
    • 宋元徽二年 - A specific year in the Southern Dynasties period, indicating the historical context.
    • 七月中，于昏夕闲 - Refers to the evening or dusk in the seventh month, a time of leisure.
    • 闲卧未寝，叹念万品死生，流转无定 - Expresses a deep contemplation of the impermanence of life and death.
    • 自惟已身，将从何来 - Means to reflect on oneself and wonder where one comes from.
    • 一心祈念，冀通感梦 - Means to pray with a focused mind, hoping for a dream that conveys a message.
    • 时夕结阴，室无灯烛 - Describes a cloudy evening with no lighting in the room.
    • 有顷，见枕边如萤火者，冏然明照，流飞而去 - Describes seeing a bright light that flies away from the pillow.
    • 俄而一室尽明，爰至空中，有如朝昼 - Describes the room becoming bright and the light reaching the sky, as if it were daytime.
    • 秀远遽起坐，合掌端念 - Describes Xiu Yuan quickly sitting up and meditating with folded hands.
    • 顷，见中宁四五丈上，有一桥阁焉 - Describes seeing a bridge and a pavilion at a height of several feet.
    • 又阑槛朱彩，立于空中 - Describes red decorations standing in the sky.
    • 秀远了不觉升动之时，而已自见平坐桥侧 - Describes Xiu Yuan suddenly realizing he is sitting on the side of the bridge without knowing how he got there.
    • 见桥上士女，往返填衢，衣服妆束，不异世人 - Describes seeing men and women on the bridge, their clothing and appearance no different from ordinary people.
    • 末有一妪，年可三十许，上着青袄，下服白布裳 - Describes an old woman appearing, about thirty years old, with a blue jacket and white cloth skirt.
    • 行至秀远左边而立 - Describes the old woman standing to the left of Xiu Yuan.
    • 有顷，复有一妇人，通体衣白布，为偏环髻，手持华香，当前而立 - Describes another woman appearing, dressed in white cloth, with a partial ring hairstyle, holding flowers and incense, standing in front of Xiu Yuan.
    • 语秀远曰：“汝欲睹前身，即我是也，以此华供养佛故，故得转身作汝。” - Describes the dialogue where the woman says, 'If you want to see your previous life, I am it. Because I offered these flowers to the Buddha, I was reborn as you.'
    • 回指白妪曰：“此即复是我先身也。” - Describes the woman pointing to the white-clad old woman and saying, 'This is also my previous life.'
    • 言毕而去，去后桥亦渐隐 - Describes the women finishing speaking and leaving, with the bridge gradually disappearing.
    • 秀远忽然不觉还下之时，光亦寻灭也 - Describes Xiu Yuan suddenly realizing he is back on the ground, with the light also disappearing.
    • 珠林三十二广记一百十四 - Indicates the source of the text, from the *Jade Forest* (珠林) volume 32 and the *Record of the Broadly Recorded* (广记) volume 114.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 三宝 - Refers to the Three Jewels of Buddhism: Buddha, Dharma, and Sangha.
    • 耳顺 - A term from Confucius, meaning to reach the age of 60, when one can listen to others' words without anger.
    • 昏夕闲 - Refers to the evening or dusk, a time of leisure and reflection.
    • 万品死生，流转无定 - Expresses the Buddhist concept of impermanence and the cycle of rebirth.
    • 桥阁 - A bridge and a pavilion, often used in Chinese literature to symbolize a transition or a place of enlightenment.
    • 士女 - Refers to men and women, often used to describe a diverse group of people.
    • 前身 - Refers to a previous life, a concept central to Buddhist belief in reincarnation.
    • 华香 - Flowers and incense, often used in religious contexts to symbolize offerings to the Buddha.

    Proper Nouns

    • 宋陈秀远 - The name of the subject, Cen Xiu Yuan.
    • 颍川 - The place of origin, Yen-chuan.
    • 宋元徽二年 - A specific year in the Southern Dynasties period.
    • 珠林三十二广记一百十四 - The source of the text, from the *Jade Forest* (珠林) volume 32 and the *Record of the Broadly Recorded* (广记) volume 114.

    Cen Xiu Yuan, a native of Yen-chuan, once served as the Western Censor of Xiangzhou and resided as a guest in Linxiang County. In his youth, he revered the Three Jewels of Buddhism, and even after reaching the age of sixty, he remained steadfast in his occupation. In the second year of the Yuexiu era of the Song Dynasty, during the seventh month, on an evening when he was lying down without sleeping, he reflected on the impermanence of life and death, pondering where he had come from. With a focused mind, he prayed, hoping for a dream that would convey a message. That evening was overcast, and the room was dark without any lighting. After a while, he saw a light like a firefly beside his pillow, shining brightly and then flying away. Soon, the entire room was illuminated, and the light reached the sky, as if it were daytime. Cen Xiu Yuan quickly sat up, folded his hands, and meditated. After a while, he saw a bridge and a pavilion about ten to fifteen feet above him, with red decorations standing in the sky. He did not realize how he was lifted, but he found himself sitting on the side of the bridge. He saw men and women on the bridge, coming and going, their clothing and appearance no different from ordinary people. An old woman, about thirty years old, appeared, wearing a green coat and a white cloth skirt, and stood to the left of Cen Xiu Yuan. After a while, another woman appeared, dressed entirely in white cloth, with a partial ring hairstyle, holding flowers and incense, and stood in front of him. She said to Cen Xiu Yuan, "If you want to see your previous life, I am it. Because I offered these flowers to the Buddha, I was reborn as you." She then pointed to the white-clad old woman and said, "This is also my previous life." After speaking, she left, and the bridge gradually disappeared. Cen Xiu Yuan suddenly realized he was back on the ground, and the light also disappeared. Jade Forest volume 32, Record of the Broadly Recorded volume 114. [146]

    宋沙门智达者，益州索寺僧也，行颇流俗，而善经呗。年二十三，宋元徽三年六月病死，身暖不殓，遂经二日稍还，至三日旦，而能言视。自说言：始困之时，见两人皆着黄布裤褶，一人立于户外，一人迳造床前，曰：“上人应去，可下地也。”达曰：“贫道作羸，不堪涉道。”此人复曰：“可乘轝也。”言卒而轝至，达既升之，意识恍然，不复见家人屋及所乘轝。四望极目，但睹荒野途迳艰危，示道登蹑之，不得休息。至于朱门，墙闼甚华，达入至堂下。 [147]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [147]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 宋沙门智达者 - Refers to a monk named Zhi Da from the Song dynasty.
    • 益州索寺僧也 - Indicates that Zhi Da was a monk from the Suo Temple in Yizhou (present-day Chengdu, Sichuan).
    • 行颇流俗 - Suggests that Zhi Da's behavior was somewhat conventional or unremarkable.
    • 善经呗 - Means he was skilled in reciting scriptures and chants.
    • 身暖不殓 - Means his body remained warm, delaying the burial.
    • 遂经二日稍还 - Indicates that after two days, he began to show signs of recovery.
    • 至三日旦，而能言视 - Means on the third day morning, he could speak and see again.
    • 上人应去，可下地也 - The phrase '上人' is a respectful term for a monk, and this statement suggests that the monk should leave.
    • 贫道作羸，不堪涉道 - '贫道' is a humble term for a monk, and '作羸' means weak or frail, indicating that the monk felt too weak to travel.
    • 可乘轝也 - Suggests that he could ride in a carriage.
    • 言卒而轝至 - The carriage arrived immediately after the suggestion.
    • 达既升之，意识恍然 - After getting into the carriage, Zhi Da's consciousness became unclear.
    • 不复见家人屋及所乘轝 - He no longer saw his family's house or the carriage.
    • 四望极目，但睹荒野途迳艰危 - From all directions, he only saw a desolate wilderness with difficult paths.
    • 示道登蹑之，不得休息 - The path showed him to climb and walk without rest.
    • 至于朱门，墙闼甚华 - He reached a red gate with very ornate walls and doors.
    • 达入至堂下 - Zhi Da entered the hall.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 宋 - Refers to the Southern Song dynasty (479-589 AD).
    • 元徽三年 - The third year of the Yuangui era (475 AD) during the reign of Emperor Wu of Song.
    • 益州 - An ancient Chinese province, roughly corresponding to modern-day Sichuan.
    • 索寺 - A specific temple in Yizhou (Chengdu).
    • 黄布裤褶 - Yellow cloth trousers and jackets, typical attire for certain officials or monks.
    • 上人 - A respectful term for a monk.
    • 贫道 - A humble term for a monk.
    • 轝 - A type of carriage or palanquin.
    • 朱门 - A red gate, often associated with wealth and nobility.
    • 墙闼甚华 - Ornate walls and doors, indicating a grand and luxurious setting.

    Proper Nouns

    • 宋 - Southern Song dynasty.
    • 元徽三年 - The third year of the Yuangui era (475 AD).
    • 益州 - Ancient province, modern-day Sichuan.
    • 索寺 - Suo Temple in Yizhou (Chengdu).
    • 智达 - The name of the monk.
    • 黄布裤褶 - Yellow cloth trousers and jackets.
    • 上人 - Respectful term for a monk.
    • 贫道 - Humble term for a monk.
    • 轝 - Carriage or palanquin.
    • 朱门 - Red gate.
    • 墙闼 - Walls and doors.

    A Monk Named Zhi Da from the Song Dynasty

    Zhi Da was a monk from the Suo Temple in Yizhou (present-day Chengdu, Sichuan). His behavior was somewhat conventional, but he was skilled in reciting scriptures and chants. At the age of twenty-three, in the third month of the Yuangui era of the Song dynasty (475 AD), he fell ill and died. His body remained warm, delaying the burial. After two days, he began to show signs of recovery. On the third day morning, he could speak and see again. He recounted his experience: when he first became ill, he saw two people dressed in yellow cloth trousers and jackets. One stood outside the door, and the other came directly to the bedside. The person said, "Reverend, you should leave; get off the bed." Zhi Da replied, "I am weak and frail; I cannot travel." The person then said, "You can ride in a carriage." As soon as he spoke, the carriage arrived. Zhi Da got into it, and his consciousness became unclear. He no longer saw his family's house or the carriage. Looking around, he only saw a desolate wilderness with difficult paths. The path showed him to climb and walk without rest. He reached a red gate with very ornate walls and doors. Zhi Da entered the hall. [147]

    堂上有一贵人，朱衣冠帻，据床傲坐，姿貌严远，甚有威容，左右兵卫百许人，皆朱拄刀，列直森然。贵人见达，乃敛颜正色谓曰：“出家之人，何宜多过？”达曰：“有识以来不忆作罪。”问曰：“诵戒废不？”达曰：“初受具足之时，实常习诵，比逐斋讲，恒事转经，故于诵戒，时有亏废。”复曰：“沙门时不诵戒，此非罪何为？可且诵经！”达即诵法华，三契而止。贵人敕所录达使人曰：“可送置恶地，勿令太苦。”二人引达将去，行数十里，稍闻轰○闹声沸火，而前路转闇。 [148]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [148]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 据床傲坐 - Sitting arrogantly on the bed, indicating a haughty posture.
    • 姿貌严远 - Describes the noble person's appearance as severe and distant.
    • 甚有威容 - Had a very dignified and imposing presence.
    • 列直森然 - Stood straight and orderly, emphasizing discipline and order.
    • 三契而止 - Recited three times and stopped.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 堂上 - Refers to the main hall, often used in formal settings.
    • 贵人 - Refers to a noble or high-ranking person.
    • 朱衣冠帻 - Describes the noble person's attire, which is significant in traditional Chinese culture.
    • 出家之人 - Refers to a monk or someone who has renounced the world.
    • 诵戒 - Refers to the recitation of Buddhist precepts.
    • 恶地 - Refers to a bad or undesirable place, often used in Buddhist contexts.

    In the hall, there was a noble person wearing a red cap and headband, sitting arrogantly on the bed with a severe and distant appearance, having a very dignified and imposing presence. About a hundred guards stood straight and orderly on either side, all holding red-handled swords. The noble person saw the monk and then adopted a serious expression, saying, "Why should a monk have many faults?" The monk replied, "Since gaining consciousness, I do not remember committing any sins." The noble person asked, "Have you neglected the recitation of the precepts?" The monk answered, "When I first received the full ordination, I indeed regularly recited them. In recent times, I have been attending sermons and reciting scriptures, thus there have been occasional lapses in reciting the precepts." The noble person said, "You may recite the scriptures instead." The monk then recited the Lotus Sutra three times and stopped. The noble person instructed those in charge, "You may send him to a bad place, but do not make it too harsh." Two people led the monk away, traveling for dozens of miles, and gradually heard a loud and chaotic noise, while the road ahead became darker. [148]

    次至一门，高数十丈，色甚坚黑，盖铁门也，墙亦如之。达心自念：经说地狱，此其是矣。乃大恐怖。悔在世时，不修业行。及大门里，闹声转壮，久之靖听，方知是人叫呼之响，门里转闇，无所复见。时火光乍灭乍扬，见有数人，反缚前行，后有数人，执○○之，血流如泉；其一人乃达从伯母，彼此相见，意欲共语，有人曳之殊疾，不遑得言。入门二百许步，见有一物，形如米囤，可高丈余，二人执达，掷置囤上，囤里有火，焰烧达身，半体皆烂，痛不可忍，自囤坠地，闷绝良久。二人复将达去。 [149]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [149]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 经说地狱 - Refers to the Buddhist concept of hell as described in scriptures.
    • 业行 - Refers to actions and deeds that have karmic consequences.
    • 反缚 - Refers to being bound with hands tied behind the back, a form of restraint.
    • 血流如泉 - A vivid description of blood flowing profusely, indicating severe injury or torture.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 地狱 - Refers to the Buddhist concept of hell, a place of punishment for evil deeds.
    • 铁门 - Refers to a gate made of iron, symbolizing a barrier to the afterlife.
    • 米囤 - Refers to a grain silo, a common storage structure in ancient China.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • The long descriptive sentence describing the gate and wall must be read as a single unit.
    • The internal monologue is a direct quote, and the colon introduces the thought.
    • The sentence structure is simple but conveys a strong emotional response.
    • The sentence structure is straightforward but conveys a growing sense of dread.
    • The sentence structure is clear but conveys a moment of realization.
    • The sentence structure is simple but conveys a sense of increasing darkness and fear.
    • The sentence structure is simple but conveys a sense of flickering light and danger.
    • The sentence structure is complex but conveys a series of events and actions.
    • The sentence structure is complex but conveys a series of interactions and reactions.
    • This long sequence must be read as a series of actions and events, each contributing to the overall narrative.

    Proper Nouns

    • 达 - The protagonist of the story.
    • 从伯母 - Da's aunt, a specific character in the narrative.
    Translation
    Next, he reached a gate, towering several dozen feet high, very dark and black, indicating it was made of iron. The wall was also similar in appearance. Da thought to himself: "The scriptures speak of hell, this must be it." He became greatly terrified. He regretted not cultivating virtue and good deeds in his lifetime. As he entered the gate, the noise grew louder. After a while, upon quiet listening, he realized it was the sound of people calling out. Inside the gate, it grew darker, and he could no longer see anything. The firelight flickered on and off. He saw several people bound and being led forward, behind them were several people holding something, and blood flowed like a spring. One of them was Da's aunt. They saw each other and wanted to speak, but someone dragged him away so quickly that he had no time to speak. After entering the gate for about two hundred steps, he saw an object shaped like a grain silo, about ten feet tall. Two people grabbed Da and threw him onto the silo. Inside the silo was fire, which burned Da's body. Half of his body was burned to a crisp, and the pain was unbearable. He fell from the silo to the ground and fainted for a long time. Two people took Da away again.
    [149]

    见有铁镬十余，皆煮罪人，人在镬中，随沸出没，镬侧有人，以○刺之，或有攀镬出者，两目沸凸，舌出尺余，肉尽炘烂而犹不死。诸镬皆满，唯有一镬尚空，二人谓达曰：“上人即时应入此中。”达闻其言，肝涂地，乃请之曰：“君听贫道，一得礼佛，便至心稽首，愿免此苦。”伏地食顷，祈悔特至。既而四望，无所复见，唯睹平原茂树，风景清明。而二人犹导达行，至一楼下，楼形高小，上有人裁得容坐，谓达曰：“沙门现受轻报，殊可欣也。”达于楼下，忽然不觉还就身时。达今犹存在索寺也，斋戒愈坚，禅诵弥固。珠林九十 [150]

    [Translation]
    There are over ten iron cauldrons, all boiling criminals. The criminals are in the cauldrons, rising and sinking with the boiling water. By the side of the cauldrons, there are people who use an unknown tool to pierce them. Some climb out of the cauldron, their eyes bulging and tongues protruding a foot or more, their flesh burnt to a crisp but they still do not die. All the cauldrons are full, except for one that is still empty. Two men tell the monk, "The monk should immediately enter this one." The monk, hearing their words, feels his liver on the ground, and then pleads with them, "Please listen to me, as soon as I bow to the Buddha, I will sincerely bow my head, hoping to avoid this suffering." For a moment, he lies on the ground, praying and repenting deeply. Looking around, he sees nothing else, only a plain with lush trees and clear scenery. The two men still guide the monk, leading him to a small high tower. There is just enough space for someone to sit, and they say to the monk, "The monk is currently receiving a light punishment, which is quite pleasant." The monk suddenly does not feel like he is in his body anymore. The monk still exists at Suxi Temple, his fasting and meditation becoming even more steadfast. [150]

    宋袁廓，字思度，陈郡人也。元徽中，为吴郡丞，病经少日，奄然如死，但余息未尽，棺含之具并备，待毕而殓，三日而能转动视瞬。自说云：有使者称教唤，廓随去，既至，有大城池，楼堞高整，阶闼崇丽。既命廓进，主人南面，阶陛森然，威饰冠首。执刀者点廓坐，坐定，温凉毕，设酒炙果粽菹肴等，廓皆尝进，种族形味，不异世中。酒数行，主人谓廓曰：“身至簿不幸合任有阙，以君才颖，故欲相屈，当能顾怀不？”廓意亦知是幽途，乃固辞：凡薄非所克堪，家少穷孤，兄弟零落公私二三，乞蒙恩放。主人曰：“君当以幽显异方，故有辞耳。此间荣群资待，身口服御，乃当胜君世中勤勤之怀。甚贪共事，想必降意，副所期也。”廓复固请曰：“男女藐然，并在龆龀，仆一旦恭任，养视无托，父子之恋理有可矜。”廓因流涕稽颡。主人曰：“君辞让乃尔，何容相逼？愿言不获，深为叹恨。 [151]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [151]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 奄然如死 - Literally means 'suddenly like death,' indicating a state of apparent death.
    • 但余息未尽 - Means 'but the breath is not completely gone,' suggesting that the person is still alive but in a critical condition.
    • 棺含之具并备 - Means 'all the funeral preparations are ready,' indicating that the funeral arrangements have been completed.
    • 待毕而殓 - Means 'wait until the preparations are complete before embalming,' indicating a delay in the funeral process.
    • 三日而能转动视瞬 - Means 'after three days, he was able to move and blink,' indicating a gradual recovery.
    • 有使者称教唤 - Means 'there was a messenger who called,' suggesting a divine or supernatural call.
    • 大城池 - Refers to a large city or fortress.
    • 楼堞高整 - Means 'towers and battlements are high and orderly,' describing the city's defenses.
    • 阶闼崇丽 - Means 'stairs and gates are grand and beautiful,' describing the city's architecture.
    • 执刀者点廓坐 - Means 'a person with a knife pointed at Kuò to sit,' indicating a formal or ceremonial seating arrangement.
    • 温凉毕 - Means 'warm and cool dishes are served,' indicating a meal.
    • 设酒炙果粽菹肴等 - Means 'serving wine, roasted meat, fruits, zongzi (sticky rice dumplings), pickled vegetables, and other dishes,' indicating a variety of food.
    • 种族形味 - Means 'various types and flavors,' indicating a diverse selection of food.
    • 不异世中 - Means 'no different from the world,' indicating that the food is familiar and similar to what is found in the living world.
    • 酒数行 - Means 'several rounds of wine,' indicating multiple servings of wine.
    • 身至簿不幸合任有阙 - Means 'my position is humble and unfortunate, lacking in duties,' indicating a modest and underappreciated role.
    • 以君才颖 - Means 'because of your sharp talent,' indicating that the person has exceptional abilities.
    • 故欲相屈 - Means 'therefore, I wish to invite you,' indicating a request or invitation.
    • 当能顾怀不 - Means 'can you consider it?' indicating a question about whether the person will accept the invitation.
    • 凡薄非所克堪 - Means 'my humble position is not suitable,' indicating that the person feels unworthy of the role.
    • 家少穷孤 - Means 'my family is poor and has few members,' indicating financial and familial difficulties.
    • 兄弟零落公私二三 - Means 'my brothers are scattered in both public and private life,' indicating a lack of support from family and friends.
    • 乞蒙恩放 - Means 'I beg for your mercy and release,' indicating a request for leniency.
    • 幽途 - Means 'the afterlife' or 'the underworld,' indicating a reference to the spiritual realm.
    • 此间荣群资待 - Means 'in this place, there are many honors and resources,' indicating the potential benefits of the afterlife.
    • 身口服御 - Means 'my body and clothing are well cared for,' indicating the quality of life in the afterlife.
    • 乃当胜君世中勤勤之怀 - Means 'it will be better than your hard work in the world,' indicating that the afterlife offers greater rewards.
    • 甚贪共事 - Means 'I very much desire to work together,' indicating a strong interest in collaboration.
    • 想必降意 - Means 'I hope you will lower your expectations,' indicating a request for understanding.
    • 副所期也 - Means 'to meet my expectations,' indicating a desire for the person to fulfill the role.
    • 男女藐然 - Means 'my children are still young,' indicating that the person has young children.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 元徽中 - A time period during the Southern Dynasties, specifically the reign of Emperor Yuan of Song (472-477).

    Proper Nouns

    • 紹興 - Era name of the Southern Song; rendered as Shaoxing.

    In the Song Dynasty, there was a man named Yuan Kuo, whose courtesy name was Sishu, and he was from Chen Prefecture. During the reign of Emperor Yuan of Song (472-477), he served as the magistrate of Wu Prefecture. He fell ill for a short time and appeared to be dead, but his breath was not completely gone. All the funeral preparations were ready, and they waited until the preparations were complete before embalming him. After three days, he was able to move and blink. He recounted that a messenger came and called him, and Kuo followed him. Upon arrival, they saw a large city with high and orderly towers and battlements, and grand and beautiful stairs and gates. Once he was invited to enter, the host sat facing south, with a solemn and majestic appearance adorned with a crown. A person with a knife pointed at Kuo to sit, and once seated, warm and cool dishes were served, including wine, roasted meat, fruits, zongzi (sticky rice dumplings), pickled vegetables, and other dishes. Kuo tried all the food, which had various types and flavors, no different from what is found in the world. After several rounds of wine, the host said to Kuo, "My position is humble and unfortunate, lacking in duties. Because of your sharp talent, I wish to invite you. Can you consider it?" Kuo understood that this was a reference to the afterlife and firmly declined, saying, "My humble position is not suitable. My family is poor and has few members, and my brothers are scattered in both public and private life. I beg for your mercy and release." The host replied, "You are declining because the afterlife is different from the world. Here, there are many honors and resources, and my body and clothing are well cared for, which will be better than your hard work in the world. I very much desire to work together, and I hope you will lower your expectations to meet my expectations." Kuo again firmly declined, saying, "My children are still young, and if I accept this role, there will be no one to care for them. The affection between father and son is something that can be pitied." Kuo then wept and bowed. The host said, "Your refusal is so sincere, how can I force you? If I cannot persuade you, I deeply regret it." [151]

    ”就案上取一卷文书拘黵之。既而廓谢恩辞归，主人曰：“君不欲定省先亡乎？”乃遣人将廓行，经历寺署甚众，末得一垣，城门楣并，盖囹圄也。将廓入中，斜趣一隅，有诸屋宇，骈填衔接，而甚陋弊。次有一屋，见其所生母羊氏在此屋中，容服不佳，甚异平生，见廓惊喜。户边有一人，身面伤痍，形类甚异，呼廓语，廓惊问其谁？羊氏谓廓曰：“此王夫人，汝不识耶？”王夫人曰：“吾在世时，不信报应，虽复无甚余罪，正坐鞭挞婢仆过苦，故受此罚。亡来楚毒，殆无暂休，今特少时宽隙耳。前唤汝姊来，望以自代，竟无所益，徒为忧聚。”言毕涕泗，王夫人即廓嫡母也。廓姊时亦在其侧。有顷，使人复将廓去，经涉巷陌，闾里整顿，似是民居。末有一宅竹篱茅屋，见父披被着巾，?案而坐。廓入门，父扬手遣廓曰：“汝即蒙罢，可速归去，不须来也。廓跪辞而归，使人送廓至家而去。廓今太子洗马是也。”珠林五十二 [152]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [152]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 就案上取一卷文书拘黵之 - Takes a document from the table and imposes some form of restraint or confinement on someone.
    • 定省先亡 - Ritual act of visiting or paying respects to one's deceased parents.
    • 盖囹圄也 - Clarifies that the place they are led to is a prison.
    • 楚毒 - Refers to severe suffering or torture.
    • 殆无暂休 - Indicates that the suffering is almost continuous.
    • 望以自代 - Hoping to replace oneself or take one's place.
    • 徒为忧聚 - Suggests that the hope was futile and only caused more worry.
    • 太子洗马 - A title, specifically a position in the imperial court, literally 'to wash the horse,' a euphemism for a high-ranking official.
    • 珠林五十二 - Refers to a specific section or chapter in a larger work, likely a collection of stories or anecdotes.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 廓 - Likely a name, possibly a historical figure or a character in a story.
    • 王夫人 - Refers to a woman named Wang, likely a noblewoman or a significant figure in the story.
    • 嫡母 - Refers to a stepmother, specifically the wife of one's father who is not the biological mother.
    • 珠林五十二 - A reference to a specific section or chapter in a larger work, likely a collection of stories or anecdotes. '珠林' (zhū lín) can be translated as 'Jade Forest' or 'Pearl Grove,' and '五十二' (wǔ shí èr) means 'fifty-two.'

    Proper Nouns

    • 廓 - Likely a name, possibly a historical figure or a character in a story.
    • 羊氏 - The name of Kuò's biological mother.
    • 王夫人 - The name of a woman who is identified as Wang's wife.
    • 太子洗马 - A title, specifically a position in the imperial court.
    • 珠林五十二 - A reference to a specific section or chapter in a larger work, likely a collection of stories or anecdotes.

    From the table, a document was taken and used to detain someone. Afterward, Kuò expressed gratitude and declined to return. The host asked, "Do you not wish to pay respects to your deceased parents?" Then, a person was sent to lead Kuò away, passing through many temples and offices. They finally reached a wall, with the city gate and lintel aligned, which was a prison. Kuò was led inside and moved to a corner, where there were several buildings closely packed and connected, but in poor condition. In one of these buildings, Kuò saw his biological mother, Yang Shi, who was dressed poorly and looked very different from her usual self. She was surprised and delighted to see Kuò. Near the door, there was a person with visible injuries and a strange appearance who called out to Kuò. Kuò was startled and asked who she was. Yang Shi said to Kuò, "This is Wang's wife, do you not recognize her?" Wang's wife said, "When I was alive, I did not believe in retribution. Although I had no major sins, I was punished for excessively whipping my servants and maids. Since my death, I have suffered greatly, almost without a moment's rest. Now, I am only experiencing a brief respite. I previously called your sister to come, hoping she would take my place, but it was of no benefit and only caused more worry." She finished speaking and wept. Wang's wife was Kuò's stepmother. At that time, Kuò's sister was also present. After a while, the person who had led Kuò away brought him back, passing through streets and alleys that seemed to be residential areas. Finally, they reached a house with a bamboo fence and a thatched roof, where Kuò's father was sitting with a blanket and a headscarf, looking at a table. Kuò entered the house, and his father waved his hand, saying, "You are now released, go back quickly, do not come again." Kuò knelt and respectfully departed. The person who had led him was sent back home. Kuò is now a high-ranking official in the imperial court, known as the Imperial Horse Washer. This is recorded in the "Jade Forest, Chapter Fifty-Two." [152]

    宋韩徽者，未详何许人也，居于支江，其叔幼宗，宋末为湘州府中兵。升明元年，荆州刺史沈攸之举兵东下，湘府长史庾佩玉阻甲自守，未知所赴；以幼宗猜贰，杀之，戮及妻孥，徽以兄子系于郡狱，铁木竟体，钳梏甚严，须考毕情党，将悉诛灭。徽惶迫无计，待斯而已。徽本尝事佛，颇讽读观世音经，于是昼夜诵经至数百遍。方昼，而锁忽自鸣，若烧炮石瓦爆吒之声，已而视其锁，鏙然自解。徽惧狱司谓其解截，遽呼告之，吏虽惊异，而犹更钉鍱。徽如常讽诵，又经一日，锁复鸣解，状如初时。吏乃具告佩玉，玉取锁详视，服其通感，即免释之。徽今尚在，勤业殊至。珠林二十七 [153]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [153]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 未詳何許人也 - Classical phrase meaning 'it is unknown where he came from' or 'his origin is unclear.'
    • 支江 - Place name, likely referring to a location in ancient China, possibly in the Jiangxi or Hunan region.
    • 湘州府中兵 - Refers to a military officer in the government of Xiang Prefecture, specifically serving in the military department.
    • 升明元年 - The first year of the Shengming era, corresponding to 477 CE in the Southern Qi Dynasty.
    • 荆州刺史沈攸之 - Refers to Shen Youzhi, the governor of Jing Prefecture, who led a rebellion against the Southern Qi Dynasty.
    • 湘府長史庾佩玉 - Refers to Yu Peiyu, the chief secretary of the Xiang Prefecture government.
    • 阻甲自守 - Means to defend oneself with armor, implying a defensive stance.
    • 未知所赴 - Means 'did not know whom to follow' or 'was unsure of his allegiance.'
    • 以幼宗猜贰 - Means 'because of suspicion of disloyalty toward his younger brother.'
    • 戮及妻孥 - Means 'killed his wife and children' or 'his entire family was executed.'
    • 徽以兄子系於郡獄 - Means 'Han Hui, his nephew, was imprisoned in the prefectural jail.'
    • 鐵木竟體 - Means 'his body was covered with iron and wood,' referring to the severe shackles he was bound with.
    • 鉗梏甚嚴 - Means 'the shackles were very tight and severe.'
    • 考畢情黨 - Means 'after interrogating his associates' or 'after investigating his connections.'
    • 將悉誅滅 - Means 'intended to kill all of them.'
    • 惶迫無計 - Means 'in a state of panic and without any plan.'
    • 待斯而已 - Means 'there was nothing else to do but wait.'
    • 頗諷讀觀世音經 - Means 'he often recited the *Guanshiyin Jing* (Scripture of the Bodhisattva of Compassion).'
    • 晝夜誦經至數百遍 - Means 'he recited the scripture day and night, hundreds of times.'
    • 鎖忽自鳴 - Means 'the lock suddenly made a noise.'
    • 若燒炮石瓦爆吒之聲 - Means 'like the sound of exploding stones or tiles when they are burned.'
    • 鏙然自解 - Means 'it opened by itself.'
    • 懼獄司謂其解截 - Means 'he was afraid that the jailer would think he had tampered with the lock.'
    • 遽呼告之 - Means 'he quickly called out to the jailer to explain.'
    • 吏雖驚異 - Means 'the official was surprised but still.'
    • 更釘鍱 - Means 're-fastened the shackles.'
    • 又經一日 - Means 'after another day.'
    • 吏乃具告佩玉 - Means 'the official then reported everything to Yu Peiyu.'
    • 玉取鎖詳視 - Means 'Yu Peiyu took the lock and examined it carefully.'
    • 服其通感 - Means 'he was convinced by the supernatural phenomenon.'
    • 即免釋之 - Means 'and immediately released him.'
    • 勤業殊至 - Means 'he diligently pursued his career.'
    • 珠林二十七 - Refers to the *Zhu Lin* (Treasury of Jewels), specifically volume 27.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 宋韓徽 - Refers to Han Hui, a figure from the Southern Qi Dynasty.
    • 宋末 - Refers to the end of the Southern Song Dynasty, which is a historical context for the story.
    • 湘州 - Refers to Xiang Prefecture, a region in present-day Hunan Province.
    • 荊州 - Refers to Jing Prefecture, a region in present-day Hubei Province.
    • 沈攸之 - Refers to Shen Youzhi, a historical figure who led a rebellion against the Southern Qi Dynasty.
    • 庾佩玉 - Refers to Yu Peiyu, a historical figure who served as the chief secretary of the Xiang Prefecture government.
    • 觀世音經 - Refers to the *Guanshiyin Jing* (Scripture of the Bodhisattva of Compassion), a Buddhist text.

    Proper Nouns

    • 宋韓徽 - Han Hui
    • 宋末 - End of the Southern Song Dynasty
    • 湘州 - Xiang Prefecture
    • 荊州 - Jing Prefecture
    • 沈攸之 - Shen Youzhi
    • 庾佩玉 - Yu Peiyu
    • 觀世音經 - *Guanshiyin Jing* (Scripture of the Bodhisattva of Compassion)
    • 珠林二十七 - *Zhu Lin* (Treasury of Jewels), volume 27

    Han Hui of the Song Dynasty, whose origins are unknown, lived in Zhijiang. His uncle, Youzong, served as a military officer in the government of Xiang Prefecture at the end of the Southern Song Dynasty. In the first year of the Shengming era, Governor Shen Youzhi of Jing Prefecture led an army eastward. Yu Peiyu, the chief secretary of Xiang Prefecture, defended himself with armor but did not know whom to follow. Suspecting disloyalty from Youzong, he had him executed along with his wife and children. Han Hui, being Youzong's nephew, was imprisoned in the prefectural jail, where he was bound with iron and wood, his shackles being very tight and severe. They intended to interrogate his associates and kill all of them. Han Hui was in a state of panic and had no plan; there was nothing else to do but wait. Han Hui had previously been a Buddhist and often recited the Guanshiyin Jing. He recited the scripture day and night, hundreds of times. One day, the lock on his cell suddenly made a noise, like the sound of exploding stones or tiles when they are burned. When he looked at the lock, it opened by itself. Han Hui was afraid that the jailer would think he had tampered with the lock, so he quickly called out to explain. The official was surprised but still re-fastened the shackles. Han Hui continued to recite the scripture as usual, and after another day, the lock again made a noise and opened by itself, just as it had before. The official then reported everything to Yu Peiyu. Yu Peiyu took the lock and examined it carefully, convinced by the supernatural phenomenon, and immediately released Han Hui. Han Hui is still alive today, diligently pursuing his career. This story is recorded in the Zhu Lin, volume 27. [153]

    宋释慧严京师东安寺僧也，理思该畅，见器道俗。尝嫌大涅槃经文字繁多，遂加刊削，就成数卷，写两三通，以示同好。因寝寤之际，忽见一人，身长二丈余，形气伟壮，谓之曰：“涅槃尊经，众藏之宗，何得以君璅思，轻加斟酌？”严怅然不释，犹以发意，苟觅多知明夕将卧，复见昨人，甚有怒色，谓曰：“过而知改，是谓非过；昨故相告，犹不己乎？此经既无行理，且君祸亦将及。”严惊觉失措，未及申旦，便驰信求还，悉烧除之。尘外精舍释道俨具所谙闻也。珠林十八 [154]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [154]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 釋慧嚴 - The name of a Buddhist monk, where '釋' is a common prefix for Buddhist monks.
    • 京師東安寺僧也 - '京師' refers to the capital city, and '東安寺' is the name of a temple. '僧也' means 'is a monk.'
    • 理思該暢 - Means 'his reasoning is comprehensive and smooth.'
    • 見器道俗 - '見器' means 'to be esteemed,' and '道俗' refers to both monastics and laypeople.
    • 大涅槃經 - The title of a Buddhist scripture, *Mahāparinirvāṇa Sūtra*.
    • 文字繁多 - Means 'the text is verbose.'
    • 加刊削 - Means 'to edit or revise.'
    • 就成數卷 - Means 'to produce several volumes.'
    • 寫两三通 - Means 'to copy two or three times.'
    • 以示同好 - Means 'to show to like-minded people.'
    • 因寝寤之际 - '寝寤' refers to the state of sleeping and waking, and '际' means 'occasion.'
    • 忽见一人，身长二丈余 - '忽见' means 'suddenly saw,' and '身长二丈余' means 'a person over two zhang tall' (approximately 6.6 meters).
    • 形气伟壮 - Means 'physically imposing and strong.'
    • 谓之曰 - Means 'told him.'
    • 涅槃尊经，众藏之宗 - '涅槃尊经' means 'the revered *Mahāparinirvāṇa Sūtra*,' and '众藏之宗' means 'the root of all collections.'
    • 何得以君璅思，轻加斟酌 - '何得' means 'how can,' '璅思' means 'trivial thoughts,' and '轻加斟酌' means 'lightly edit.'
    • 严怅然不释 - '怅然' means 'disappointed,' and '不释' means 'not let go.'
    • 犹以发意 - '犹以' means 'still because of,' and '发意' means 'intention.'
    • 苟觅多知 - '苟' means 'if,' '觅' means 'seek,' and '多知' means 'many knowledges.'
    • 明夕将卧 - '明夕' means 'next night,' and '将卧' means 'about to sleep.'
    • 复见昨人 - '复见' means 'saw again,' and '昨人' means 'the person from yesterday.'
    • 甚有怒色 - '甚有' means 'very much,' and '怒色' means 'angry expression.'
    • 过而知改，是谓非过 - '过而知改' means 'to recognize and correct mistakes,' and '是谓非过' means 'that is not a mistake.'
    • 昨故相告，犹不己乎 - '昨故相告' means 'yesterday I warned you,' and '犹不己乎' means 'still not stopped?'
    • 此经既无行理 - '既无行理' means 'has no reason to be practiced.'
    • 且君祸亦将及 - '且' means 'and,' '君祸' means 'your misfortune,' and '亦将及' means 'will also come to you.'
    • 严惊觉失措 - '惊觉' means 'startled awake,' and '失措' means 'at a loss.'
    • 未及申旦 - '未及' means 'before,' and '申旦' means 'dawn.'
    • 便驰信求还 - '便' means 'then,' '驰信' means 'send a message,' and '求还' means 'request to return.'
    • 悉烧除之 - '悉' means 'all,' and '烧除之' means 'burned and destroyed.'
    • 尘外精舍释道俨具所谙闻也 - '尘外精舍' means 'a hermitage outside the world,' '释道俨' is the name of a monk, and '具所谙闻' means 'has heard of it.'
    • 珠林十八 - '珠林' is the name of a collection, and '十八' means 'eighteenth.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 釋慧嚴 - A Buddhist monk during the Southern Dynasties period.
    • 京師東安寺 - A temple located in the capital city during the Southern Dynasties.
    • 大涅槃經 - A significant Mahayana Buddhist scripture that discusses the eternal nature of the Buddha and the attainment of Nirvana.
    • 涅槃尊经，众藏之宗 - The *Mahāparinirvāṇa Sūtra* is considered a foundational text in Mahayana Buddhism.
    • 尘外精舍释道俨 - A hermitage and a monk who has heard about the incident involving Master Huaiyan.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • 釋慧嚴京師東安寺僧也 - This phrase identifies Master Huaiyan as a monk at Dong'an Temple in the capital city.
    • 理思該暢，見器道俗 - This clause describes Master Huaiyan's comprehensive and smooth reasoning and his esteem among both monastics and laypeople.
    • 大涅槃經文字繁多 - This clause states that the *Mahāparinirvāṇa Sūtra* is verbose.
    • 遂加刊削，就成數卷，寫两三通，以示同好 - This sequence describes the editing process and the copying of the edited text for like-minded people.
    • 因寝寤之际，忽见一人，身长二丈余，形气伟壮，谓之曰 - This clause describes the monk's experience of seeing a tall and imposing figure in his dream.
    • 涅槃尊经，众藏之宗，何得以君璅思，轻加斟酌 - This clause is the figure's warning about the importance of the *Mahāparinirvāṇa Sūtra* and the inappropriateness of editing it.
    • 严怅然不释，犹以发意 - This clause describes Master Huaiyan's disappointment and his continued intention to edit the text.
    • 苟觅多知明夕将卧，复见昨人，甚有怒色，谓曰 - This clause describes Master Huaiyan's intention to seek more knowledge and his subsequent dream where the figure appears again with an angry expression.
    • 过而知改，是谓非过；昨故相告，犹不己乎 - This clause is the figure's admonition about recognizing and correcting mistakes.
    • 此经既无行理，且君祸亦将及 - This clause is the figure's warning about the consequences of editing the *Mahāparinirvāṇa Sūtra*.
    • 严惊觉失措，未及申旦，便驰信求还，悉烧除之 - This clause describes Master Huaiyan's reaction and his decision to burn the edited text.
    • 尘外精舍释道俨具所谙闻也 - This clause mentions that the monk from the hermitage has heard about the incident.

    Proper Nouns

    • 釋慧嚴 - Master Huaiyan, a Buddhist monk.
    • 京師東安寺 - Dong'an Temple in the capital city.
    • 大涅槃經 - *Mahāparinirvāṇa Sūtra*.
    • 涅槃尊经 - The revered *Mahāparinirvāṇa Sūtra*.
    • 尘外精舍 - A hermitage outside the world.
    • 释道俨 - A monk who has heard about the incident.
    • 珠林十八 - The eighteenth chapter of the *Jade Forest* collection.

    Master Huaiyan, a monk at Dong'an Temple in the capital city, was known for his comprehensive and smooth reasoning, esteemed by both monastics and laypeople. He once found the Mahāparinirvāṇa Sūtra to be verbose and decided to edit it, producing several volumes and copying them two or three times to show to like-minded people. One night, while sleeping, he suddenly saw a figure over two zhang tall (approximately 6.6 meters), who appeared physically imposing and strong. The figure told him, "The Mahāparinirvāṇa Sūtra is the revered scripture and the root of all collections. How can you lightly edit it with your trivial thoughts?" Master Huaiyan felt disappointed but still held onto his intention to edit the text, hoping to seek more knowledge. The next night, as he was about to sleep, he saw the same figure again, who appeared with an angry expression and said, "To recognize and correct mistakes is not a mistake; I warned you yesterday, but you still haven't stopped? This scripture has no reason to be practiced, and your misfortune will also come to you." Startled awake and at a loss, before dawn, he sent a message to return the edited text and burned it all. The monk from the hermitage outside the world, Master Daoyan, has heard of this incident. Jade Forest, Chapter Eighteen. [154]

    宋罗玙妻费氏者，宁蜀人，父悦宋宁州刺史。费少而敬信，诵法华经，数年勤至不倦，后忽得病，苦心痛守命，阖门遑惧，属纩待时。费氏心念：我诵经勤苦，宜有善佑，庶不于此，遂致死也。既而睡卧，食顷，如寤如梦见佛于窗中，授手以摩其心，应时都愈。一堂男女婢仆，悉睹金光，亦闻香气。玙从妹即琰外族曾祖尚书中兵郎费愔之夫人也，于时省疾床前，亦具闻见。于是大兴信悟，虔戒至终，每以此瑞进化子侄焉。珠林九十五广记一百九 [155]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [155]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 诵法华经 - Recitation of the Lotus Sutra, a significant Buddhist text.
    • 数年勤至不倦 - For many years with diligence and without tiring, emphasizing persistence.
    • 阖门遑惧，属纩待时 - The entire family was in great fear, waiting anxiously for her to pass away, describing the family's distress.
    • 费氏心念 - Fei thought to herself, introducing her inner monologue.
    • 我诵经勤苦，宜有善佑，庶不于此，遂致死也 - I have recited the scriptures diligently, I should be protected by good fortune, perhaps not here, leading to death, expressing her hope and reflection.
    • 既而睡卧，食顷，如寤如梦见佛于窗中，授手以摩其心，应时都愈 - Afterwards, she fell asleep, after a meal, as if waking up or dreaming, she saw a Buddha at the window, who placed his hand on her heart, and she was immediately cured, describing the miraculous event.
    • 一堂男女婢仆，悉睹金光，亦闻香气 - All the men, women, maids, and servants in the house saw golden light and smelled a fragrant aroma, detailing the shared experience.
    • 玙从妹即琰外族曾祖尚书中兵郎费愔之夫人也，于时省疾床前，亦具闻见 - Yu's cousin was the wife of Fei Yin, a former military official and great-grandson of Yu's uncle, at that time, she visited Fei at her bedside and also witnessed everything, describing her cousin's presence and observation.
    • 于是大兴信悟，虔戒至终，每以此瑞进化子侄焉 - Thus, she greatly strengthened her faith and enlightenment, remained devout until the end, and often used this miracle to inspire her nephews and nieces, summarizing the impact of the event.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 宋 - Southern Song dynasty.
    • 宁蜀 - Ningzhou in the region of Shu (modern-day Sichuan).
    • 法华经 - The Lotus Sutra, a significant Buddhist text.
    • 属纩待时 - A traditional practice of placing a silk thread on the nose to determine if a person is breathing, indicating imminent death.
    • 一堂男女婢仆 - Refers to the entire household, including men, women, servants, and maids.
    • 玙从妹 - Yu's cousin.
    • 琰外族曾祖 - Yu's uncle's great-grandson.
    • 尚书中兵郎 - A military official in the Song dynasty.
    • 珠林 - A collection of Buddhist stories and miracles.
    • 广记 - Another collection of Buddhist stories and miracles.

    Proper Nouns

    • 宋 - Southern Song dynasty.
    • 宁蜀 - Ningzhou in the region of Shu.
    • 法华经 - The Lotus Sutra.
    • 珠林九十五 - *Zhu Lin* (Jade Forest) volume 95.
    • 广记一百九 - *Guangji* (Record of Miracles) volume 19.
    • 罗玙 - Luo Yu.
    • 费氏 - Fei.
    • 费愔 - Fei Yin.
    • 琰 - Yu's uncle.

    A Woman Named Fei, the Wife of Luo Yu of Song

    Fei was a native of Ningzhou in the region of Shu, and her father was the governor of Ningzhou during the Song dynasty. From a young age, Fei was respectful and devout, reciting the Lotus Sutra with diligence and without tiring for many years. Later, she suddenly fell ill, suffering from heart pain and clinging to life. Her entire family was in great fear, waiting anxiously for her to pass away. Fei thought to herself, "I have recited the scriptures diligently; I should be protected by good fortune. Perhaps not here, leading to death." Afterward, she fell asleep, after a meal, as if waking up or dreaming. She saw a Buddha at the window, who placed his hand on her heart, and she was immediately cured. All the men, women, maids, and servants in the house saw golden light and smelled a fragrant aroma.

    Yu's cousin, who was the wife of Fei Yin, a former military official and great-grandson of Yu's uncle, visited Fei at her bedside at that time and also witnessed everything. Thus, she greatly strengthened her faith and enlightenment, remained devout until the end, and often used this miracle to inspire her nephews and nieces. This story is recorded in the Zhu Lin (Jade Forest) volume 95 and Guangji (Record of Miracles) volume 19. [155]

    宋彭子乔者，益阳县人也，任本郡主簿，事太守沈文龙。建元元年，以罪被系，子乔，少年，尝经出家，末虽还俗，犹常诵习观世音经。时文龙盛怒，防械稍急，必欲杀之。子乔忧惧，无复余计，唯至诚诵经至百余遍。疲而昼寝，时同系者有十许人，亦俱睡卧。有湘西县吏杜道策亦系在狱，乍寐乍寤，不甚得熟。忽有双白鹤集子乔屏风上，有顷，一鹤下至子乔边，时复觉如美丽人形而已。道策起，见子乔双械脱在脚外，而械雍犹在焉。道策惊视始毕，子乔亦寤，共视械咨嗟。问子乔：有所梦不？乔曰：“不梦，”道策如向所见说之。子乔虽知必已，尚虑狱家疑其欲叛，乃解脱械雍更着。经四五日而蒙释放。琰族兄琏，亲识子乔及道策，闻二人说皆同如此。珠林二十七 [156]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [156]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 益阳县人也 - Indicates that Cao Ziqiao is from Yiyang County.
    • 任本郡主簿 - Means he served as the chief clerk in his own prefecture.
    • 事太守沈文龙 - Means he served under Prefect Shen Wenlong.
    • 建元元年 - Refers to the first year of the Jianyuan era, a reign title of Emperor Wu of Liang.
    • 以罪被系 - Means he was imprisoned due to a crime.
    • 尝经出家 - Means he had once been a monk.
    • 末虽还俗 - Means he eventually returned to lay life.
    • 犹常诵习观世音经 - Means he still regularly recited the *Guanyin Sutra*.
    • 防械稍急 - Means the guards and restraints were becoming increasingly harsh.
    • 必欲杀之 - Means they were determined to kill him.
    • 至诚诵经至百余遍 - Means he sincerely recited the sutra more than a hundred times.
    • 疲而昼寝 - Means he became tired and took a nap during the day.
    • 乍寐乍寤 - Means he would fall asleep and wake up intermittently.
    • 不甚得熟 - Means he didn't get much rest.
    • 忽有双白鹤集子乔屏风上 - Means suddenly two white cranes gathered on Cao Ziqiao's screen.
    • 一鹤下至子乔边 - Means one of the cranes flew down to Cao Ziqiao's side.
    • 时复觉如美丽人形而已 - Means sometimes he felt as if it was a beautiful human form.
    • 道策起，见子乔双械脱在脚外 - Means Du Daoce got up and saw that Cao Ziqiao's restraints were off his feet.
    • 而械雍犹在焉 - Means the restraints on his wrists were still there.
    • 子乔亦寤，共视械咨嗟 - Means Cao Ziqiao also woke up, and they both looked at the restraints in amazement.
    • 问子乔：有所梦不？ - Means someone asked Cao Ziqiao if he had dreamed anything.
    • 乔曰：“不梦” - Means Cao Ziqiao said, 'I didn't dream.'
    • 道策如向所见说之 - Means Du Daoce told him about what he had seen.
    • 子乔虽知必已 - Means Cao Ziqiao knew that the restraints would be removed.
    • 尚虑狱家疑其欲叛 - Means he still worried that the guards might suspect him of trying to escape.
    • 乃解脱械雍更着 - Means he took off the wrist restraints and put them back on.
    • 经四五日而蒙释放 - Means after four or five days, he was released.
    • 琰族兄琏，亲识子乔及道策 - Means Yan's elder brother Lian knew both Cao Ziqiao and Du Daoce.
    • 闻二人说皆同如此 - Means he heard both of their stories and they were the same.
    • 珠林二十七 - Refers to the chapter number in the *Jiu Ling* (Nine Records) text.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 益阳县 - A county in Hunan Province during the Southern and Northern Dynasties period.
    • 本郡主簿 - A local government official responsible for administrative tasks.
    • 太守沈文龙 - Prefect Shen Wenlong, a high-ranking official in the region.
    • 建元元年 - The first year of the Jianyuan era, which corresponds to AD 502.
    • 观世音经 - The *Guanyin Sutra*, a Buddhist text dedicated to Guanyin, the Bodhisattva of Compassion.
    • 双白鹤 - Two white cranes, often seen as auspicious symbols in Chinese culture.
    • 珠林二十七 - The *Jiu Ling* (Nine Records) is a collection of Buddhist stories and miracles, and this refers to the 27th chapter.

    Proper Nouns

    • 宋彭子乔 - Cao Ziqiao, the main character.
    • 益阳县 - Yiyang County.
    • 本郡主簿 - Chief clerk in his own prefecture.
    • 太守沈文龙 - Prefect Shen Wenlong.
    • 建元元年 - The first year of the Jianyuan era.
    • 观世音经 - *Guanyin Sutra*.
    • 双白鹤 - Two white cranes.
    • 杜道策 - Du Daoce, a fellow prisoner.
    • 湘西县吏 - An official from Xixiang County.
    • 琰族兄琏 - Yan's elder brother Lian.
    • 珠林二十七 - The 27th chapter of the *Jiu Ling* (Nine Records).

    Cao Ziqiao, a native of Yiyang County, served as the chief clerk in his own prefecture under Prefect Shen Wenlong. In the first year of the Jianyuan era, he was imprisoned due to a crime. Cao Ziqiao was a young man who had once been a monk. Although he eventually returned to lay life, he still regularly recited the Guanyin Sutra. At the time, Prefect Shen Wenlong was extremely angry, and the guards and restraints were becoming increasingly harsh, determined to kill him. Cao Ziqiao was filled with fear and had no other options, so he sincerely recited the sutra more than a hundred times. He became tired and took a nap during the day. At the same time, there were about ten other prisoners who were also sleeping.

    Suddenly, two white cranes gathered on Cao Ziqiao's screen. After a while, one of the cranes flew down to Cao Ziqiao's side. Sometimes he felt as if it was a beautiful human form. Du Daoce, a fellow prisoner, got up and saw that Cao Ziqiao's restraints were off his feet, but the restraints on his wrists were still there. Du Daoce was amazed and watched in disbelief. Cao Ziqiao also woke up, and they both looked at the restraints in amazement. Someone asked Cao Ziqiao if he had dreamed anything. Cao Ziqiao said, "I didn't dream." Du Daoce then told him about what he had seen.

    Cao Ziqiao knew that the restraints would be removed, but he still worried that the guards might suspect him of trying to escape. So, he took off the wrist restraints and put them back on. After four or five days, he was released. Yan's elder brother Lian, who knew both Cao Ziqiao and Du Daoce, heard their stories and found them to be the same. This account is recorded in the 27th chapter of the Jiu Ling (Nine Records). [156]

    宋董青建者，不知何许人，父字贤明，建元初为越骑校尉。初，建母宗氏孕建时，梦有人语云：“尔必生男，体上当有青志，可名为青建。”及生如言，即名焉。有容止，美言笑，性理宽和，家人未尝睹其愠色，见者咸异之。至年十四，而州迎主簿。建元初，皇储镇樊汉，为水曹参军。二年七月十六日，寝疾，自云：必不振济。至十八日临尽，起坐谓母曰：“罪尽福至，缘累永绝；愿母自割，不须忧念。”因七声大哭，声尽而绝。 [157]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [157]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 不知何許人 - Common phrase meaning 'of unknown origin' or 'of unknown whereabouts.'
    • 父字賢明 - 'Father's courtesy name was Xianming,' where '字' (courtesy name) is a formal name given to a person upon coming of age.
    • 建元初 - 'In the first year of Jianyuan,' referring to a specific era name during the Eastern Han dynasty.
    • 越骑校尉 - A military title, 'Yueqi Jiaowei,' which translates to 'Inspector General of Cavalry.'
    • 孕建时 - 'When pregnant with Jian,' referring to the time when the mother was pregnant with Jian.
    • 体上当有青志 - 'There will be a blue mark on his body,' where '青志' (qing zhi) refers to a birthmark or mole.
    • 容止 - 'Appearance and demeanor,' referring to one's physical appearance and behavior.
    • 美言笑 - 'Graceful speech and laughter,' describing someone who is charming and pleasant in conversation.
    • 性理宽和 - 'Nature is broad and harmonious,' describing a person with a gentle and easy-going temperament.
    • 家人未尝睹其愠色 - 'Family members never saw him angry,' emphasizing his calm and peaceful nature.
    • 见者咸异之 - 'Those who saw him were all surprised,' indicating that people found him remarkable.
    • 州迎主簿 - 'The state appointed him as a county clerk,' where '州' (zhou) refers to a state or region, and '主簿' (zhubu) is an official position.
    • 皇储镇樊汉 - 'The crown prince governed Fan and Han,' where '皇储' (huangchu) means 'crown prince,' and '镇' (zhen) means 'govern' or 'command.'
    • 寝疾 - 'Sick in bed,' describing someone who is ill and bedridden.
    • 罪尽福至 - 'All sins are exhausted, and blessings have arrived,' a phrase often used in Buddhist contexts to describe the end of suffering and the beginning of enlightenment.
    • 缘累永绝 - 'All karmic ties are forever severed,' another Buddhist phrase indicating the release from all negative karma.
    • 愿母自割 - 'May Mother cut herself off,' a phrase suggesting the mother should let go of her attachment to her son.
    • 不须忧念 - 'Do not worry or think about it,' advising the mother not to grieve excessively.
    • 七声大哭 - 'Cried seven times in loud sobs,' describing a series of intense cries.
    • 声尽而绝 - 'Cried until he was exhausted and died,' indicating that he passed away after crying out.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 董青建 - The name of the subject of the text, a historical figure from the Eastern Han dynasty.
    • 建元 - The era name of Emperor He of Han, indicating the time period of the events described.
    • 越骑校尉 - A high-ranking military position during the Han dynasty, responsible for cavalry units.
    • 皇储 - Refers to the crown prince, the heir apparent to the throne.
    • 镇樊汉 - Indicates the regions of Fan and Han, which were part of the Eastern Han territory.
    • 罪尽福至 - A Buddhist concept indicating the end of suffering and the beginning of enlightenment.
    • 缘累永绝 - Another Buddhist concept indicating the release from all negative karma.

    Proper Nouns

    • 董青建 - The name of the subject.
    • 賢明 - The courtesy name of Jian's father.
    • 建元 - The era name of Emperor He of Han.
    • 越骑校尉 - A military title.
    • 皇储 - The title of the crown prince.
    • 樊汉 - The regions of Fan and Han.

    Dong Qingjian of the Song Dynasty, whose origins are unknown, had a father named Xianming. In the first year of Jianyuan, he served as the Inspector General of Cavalry. Initially, when Jian's mother, Lady Zong, was pregnant with him, she had a dream in which someone told her, "You will give birth to a boy, and there will be a blue mark on his body. He can be named Qingjian." Upon his birth, just as foretold, he was named Qingjian. He had a dignified appearance and demeanor, with graceful speech and laughter. His nature was broad and harmonious, and family members never saw him angry. Those who met him were all surprised by him. By the age of fourteen, he was appointed as a county clerk by the state. In the first year of Jianyuan, the crown prince governed Fan and Han, and Qingjian served as a water administration officer. On the sixteenth day of the seventh month in the second year, he fell ill and, on his own, said that he would not recover. On the eighteenth day, as he was nearing death, he stood up and said to his mother, "All sins are exhausted, and blessings have arrived; all karmic ties are forever severed. May Mother cut herself off, do not worry or think about it." He then cried seven times in loud sobs, and cried until he was exhausted and died. [157]

    将殡丧斋前，其夜灵语云：“生死道乖，勿安斋前，自当有造像道人来迎丧者。”明日，果有道人来，名昙顺，即依灵语，向昙顺说之。昙顺曰：“贫道住在南林寺，造丈八像垂成，贤子乃有此感应。寺西有少空地，可得安葬也。”遂葬寺边。三日，其母将亲表十许人，墓所致祭，于墓东见建如生，云：“愿母割哀还去，建今还在寺住，”母即止哭而还，举家菜食长斋。至闰月十一日。贤明梦见建云：“愿父暂出东斋。”贤明便香汤自浴斋，出东斋。至十四夜，于眠中闻建唤声，惊起，建在斋前，如生时。父问：“汝住在何处？”建云：“从亡来住在练神宫中，满百日当得生忉利天。 [158]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [158]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 将殡丧斋前 - Literally means 'to carry the coffin to the front of the斋 (a place of worship or a hall for Buddhist monks).'
    • 灵语 - Refers to a spirit's message or communication.
    • 生死道乖 - Means 'the ways of life and death are different,' suggesting a separation or divergence between life and death.
    • 勿安斋前 - Means 'do not place the coffin in front of the斋.'
    • 自当有造像道人来迎丧者 - Means 'there will naturally be a sculptor monk who will come to receive the funeral.'
    • 贤子乃有此感应 - Means 'your son has this kind of感应 (sensitivity or response).'
    • 寺西有少空地 - Means 'there is a small empty space to the west of the temple.'
    • 可得安葬也 - Means 'it can be used for burial.'
    • 于墓东见建如生 - Means 'seeing Jian alive in the east of the墓 (tomb).'
    • 愿母割哀还去 - Means 'I wish my mother to cut off her sorrow and return.'
    • 建今还在寺住 - Means 'Jian is now still living in the temple.'
    • 贤明梦见建云 - Means 'Xianming dreamed of Jian saying.'
    • 愿父暂出东斋 - Means 'I wish my father to temporarily leave the东斋 (eastern斋).'
    • 香汤自浴斋 - Means 'to bathe oneself with香汤 (fragrant bath water) in the斋.'
    • 于眠中闻建唤声 - Means 'hearing Jian calling in sleep.'
    • 如生时 - Means 'as if alive.'
    • 从亡来住在练神宫中 - Means 'since death, living in the练神宫 (temple for refining spirits).'
    • 满百日当得生忉利天 - Means 'after one hundred days, I will be reborn in the忉利天 (Trāyastriṃśa Heaven).'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 斋 - Refers to a place of worship or a hall for Buddhist monks.
    • 灵语 - Refers to a spirit's message or communication, a concept found in Chinese folk beliefs.
    • 造像道人 - Refers to a monk who creates statues, often associated with Buddhist practices.
    • 练神宫 - Refers to a temple for refining spirits, a concept from Chinese religious beliefs.
    • 忉利天 - Refers to the Trāyastriṃśa Heaven, a celestial realm in Buddhist cosmology.

    Proper Nouns

    • 斋 - A place of worship or a hall for Buddhist monks.
    • 灵语 - A spirit's message or communication.
    • 造像道人 - A monk who creates statues.
    • 练神宫 - A temple for refining spirits.
    • 忉利天 - The Trāyastriṃśa Heaven.
    • 贤明 - Xianming, the father.
    • 建 - Jian, the son.
    • 昙顺 - A monk who comes to receive the funeral.

    The coffin was carried to the front of the斋, and that night, a spirit's message was heard: "The ways of life and death are different; do not place the coffin in front of the斋. There will naturally be a sculptor monk who will come to receive the funeral." The next day, a monk named Tan Shun indeed arrived, and following the spirit's message, he was told about it. Tan Shun said, "I live at Nanlin Temple, and I am almost finished creating an eighteen-foot statue. Your son has this kind of感应. There is a small empty space to the west of the temple, which can be used for burial." So, the burial was conducted at the temple. Three days later, Jian's mother, along with about ten relatives, went to the墓 to pay their respects. At the east of the墓, they saw Jian as if he were alive, saying, "I wish my mother to cut off her sorrow and return. Jian is now still living in the temple." The mother stopped crying and returned, and the whole family began to eat vegetarian food and practice long-term斋. On the eleventh day of the intercalary month, Xianming dreamed of Jian saying, "I wish my father to temporarily leave the东斋." Xianming then bathed himself with fragrant bath water in the斋 and left the东斋. On the fourteenth night, while sleeping, he heard Jian's call, and upon waking, saw Jian in front of the斋, as if he were alive. The father asked, "Where do you live?" Jian replied, "Since death, I have been living in the练神宫. After one hundred days, I will be reborn in the忉利天." [158]

    建不忍见父母兄弟哭泣伤恸，三七日礼诸佛菩萨请四天王，故得暂还。愿父母从今以后，勿复啼哭祭祀。阿母已发愿求见建，母不久当命终，即共建同生一处。父寿可得七十三，命终之后，当三年受罪报，勤苦行道，可得免脱。”问曰：汝从夜中来，那得有光明？”建曰：“今与菩萨诸天共下，此其身光耳！”又问云：“汝天上识谁？”建曰：“见王车骑张吴兴外祖宗西河。”建曰：“非但此一门中生，从四十七年以来，至今七死也生，已得四道果。先发七愿，愿生人闲，故历生死，从今永毕，得离七苦。建临尽时，见七处生死，所以大哭者，与七家分别也。 [159]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [159]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 不忍見 - Means 'could not bear to see,' expressing emotional distress.
    • 發願 - Means 'to make a vow.'
    • 三七日 - Refers to the traditional mourning period of 21 days.
    • 禮諸佛菩薩請四天王 - Means 'to offer rites to the Buddhas and Bodhisattvas and invite the Four Heavenly Kings,' a religious ritual.
    • 願 - Means 'I wish' or 'I hope.'
    • 勿復 - Means 'not to continue.'
    • 啼哭祭祀 - Refers to crying and performing rituals.
    • 求見 - Means 'to seek to see.'
    • 不久當命終 - Means 'will soon pass away.'
    • 即共建同生一处 - Means 'then Jian and I will be born together in the same place.'
    • 壽可得七十三 - Means 'can live to be seventy-three.'
    • 命終之後 - Means 'after death.'
    • 當三年受罪報 - Means 'will suffer for three years.'
    • 勤苦行道 - Means 'to diligently practice the path.'
    • 可得免脱 - Means 'can be freed.'
    • 汝从夜中来 - Means 'you come from the night.'
    • 那得有光明 - Means 'how can there be light?'
    • 今与菩萨诸天共下 - Means 'now I come down with the Bodhisattvas and heavenly beings.'
    • 此其身光耳 - Means 'this is just their body's light.'
    • 汝天上识谁 - Means 'do you recognize anyone in heaven?'
    • 见王车骑张吴兴外祖宗西河 - Describes Jian's recognition of his maternal grandfather in heaven.
    • 非但此一门中生 - Means 'not only in this family.'
    • 从四十七年以来 - Means 'since forty-seven years ago.'
    • 至今七死也生 - Means 'until now, having died and been reborn seven times.'
    • 已得四道果 - Means 'has already attained the four fruits of the path.'
    • 先发七愿 - Means 'firstly made seven vows.'
    • 愿生人闲 - Means 'to be born among humans.'
    • 故历生死 - Means 'therefore, experiencing rebirth.'
    • 从今永毕 - Means 'from now on, it is finished.'
    • 得离七苦 - Means 'can be freed from the seven sufferings.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 父母兄弟 - Refers to Jian's family members, emphasizing the close familial relationship.
    • 諸佛菩薩 - Refers to the Buddhas and Bodhisattvas, important figures in Buddhism.
    • 四天王 - Refers to the Four Heavenly Kings, guardian deities in Buddhist cosmology.
    • 阿母 - A term of endearment for mother, common in Chinese literature.
    • 父壽可得七十三 - Refers to the expected lifespan of Jian's father, which is 73 years.
    • 命終之後，當三年受罪報 - Refers to the Buddhist concept of karma and the consequences of one's actions after death.
    • 勤苦行道 - Refers to the practice of Buddhism, involving diligent effort and adherence to the path.
    • 王车骑 - A title, possibly referring to a high-ranking official in the imperial court.
    • 张吴兴 - A name, likely referring to Zhang Wuxing, a person from Wu province.
    • 外祖宗西河 - Refers to Jian's maternal grandfather from Xihé, a place name.
    • 四十七年以来 - Refers to a long period of time, spanning 47 years.
    • 四道果 - Refers to the four fruits of the path in Buddhism, representing stages of spiritual attainment.
    • 七苦 - Refers to the seven sufferings in Buddhism, including birth, aging, sickness, death, separation from loved ones, association with the disliked, and unfulfilled desires.

    Proper Nouns

    • 建 - Jian, the main character of the story.
    • 阿母 - Jian's mother.
    • 父 - Jian's father.
    • 王车骑 - A title, possibly referring to a high-ranking official.
    • 张吴兴 - A name, likely referring to Zhang Wuxing, a person from Wu province.
    • 外祖宗西河 - Jian's maternal grandfather from Xihé.
    • 諸佛菩薩 - The Buddhas and Bodhisattvas.
    • 四天王 - The Four Heavenly Kings.
    • 四道果 - The four fruits of the path in Buddhism.
    • 七苦 - The seven sufferings in Buddhism.

    Jian could not bear to see his parents and siblings crying and grieving deeply. After observing the traditional mourning period of 21 days and offering rites to the Buddhas and Bodhisattvas and inviting the Four Heavenly Kings, he was able to return briefly. He wished that his parents would stop crying and performing rituals from now on. Jian's mother had made a vow to see him, and she would soon pass away. Then, Jian and his mother would be reborn together in the same place. Jian's father could live to be seventy-three years old. After his death, he would suffer for three years, but by diligently practicing the path, he could be freed. Jian was asked, "How can there be light when you come from the night?" Jian replied, "I come down with the Bodhisattvas and heavenly beings, and this is just their body's light!" He was then asked, "Do you recognize anyone in heaven?" Jian said, "I saw my maternal grandfather, Zhang Wuxing from Xihé, who held the title of Wang Cheqi." Jian explained, "Not only in this family, but since forty-seven years ago, until now, I have been reborn seven times, and I have already attained the four fruits of the path. I first made seven vows, wishing to be born among humans, and therefore, I have experienced rebirth. From now on, it is finished, and I can be freed from the seven sufferings.
